purpose: Fast Catalog and Roster runtime projection

entries.name: Raider
entries.title: Raider
entries.description: The Pandemonium doesn't just bring out monsters—thief packs like the Rigland Raiders are known to use the chaos to their advantage.

localization.name: Attack
localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit.

localization.name: Brash Strike
localization.description: 135% damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit. Reflects 30% of the damage to the user.

entries.name: Flamby
entries.title: Flamby
entries.description: A hot-tempered bird commonly found in the plains. Although they usually prey upon insects and small rodents, they have been known to attack passersby when startled.

localization.name: Attack Two
localization.description: Damage to 2 enemies and allies gain 1 Spirit.

entries.name: Chardinal
entries.title: Chardinal
entries.description: Evolved Flambies have more powerful flames than their infant forms, and are seen as a pest by farmers due to their nasty habit of igniting crops whilst hunting for food.

localization.name: Attack Two
localization.description: Damage to 2 enemies and allies gain 1 Spirit.

localization.name: Link Burn
localization.description: 175% damage to 1 enemy if there are 3 Fire allies. 225% damage if all 4 allies are Fire.

entries.name: Bikki Punk
entries.title: Bikki Punk
entries.description: Like any region, the Kawazu Wetlands has its share of wayward youths. With the right guidance, however, they have the potential to become loyal allies.

localization.name: Attack
localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit.

localization.name: Vigor Impact
localization.description: 250% damage to 1 enemy. Usable only if the user's HP is at 75%+.

localization.name: Grant Stealth
localization.description: Grants the Stealth status effect to 1 ally. This will overwrite other status effects on that unit. Stealthed units ignore the next enemy attack that would damage more than 1 target. Any damage will remove stealth.

entries.name: Salaak
entries.title: Salaak
entries.description: Salaak are a species of semi-intelligent reptiles. Some sziviik have uprooted them from their homelands, and now keep them as servants and pets.

localization.name: Attack
localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit.

localization.name: Over Flow
localization.description: 300% damage to 1 enemy. Usable only if the user's party has 7 or more Spirit.

entries.name: Orc Ward
entries.title: Orc Ward
entries.description: Orcs choose roles by body shape and size. The most massive are chosen as guardians, who protect chieftains and are sometimes hired out as bodyguards.

localization.name: Attack
localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit.

localization.name: Over Flow
localization.description: 300% damage to 1 enemy. Usable only if the user's party has 7 or more Spirit.

entries.name: Gob Pilferer
entries.title: Gob Pilferer
entries.description: Goblins are irksome pests that like to mimic human clothing and culture. They're very bad at it. This makes their raids not just dangerous, but insulting.

localization.name: Attack
localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit.

localization.name: Dream Ender
localization.description: 500% damage to 1 enemy if they are sleeping. Allies gain 3 Spirit if the target is asleep.

localization.name: Spirit+ Revenge
localization.description: Whenever this unit is defeated by an enemy, allies gain 2 Spirit.

entries.name: Sellsword
entries.title: Sellsword
entries.description: Growing up in a rural village, she trained with the blade for this moment. Now the world's in need of a good sword, and she's ready to make her fortune.

localization.name: Attack
localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 2 Spirit.

localization.name: Survivor's Rage
localization.description: 400% damage to 1 enemy. Locked for 300TU after the user has entered the battlefield.

localization.name: Stealthy Entry
localization.description: This unit gains the Stealth status effect. Triggered when this unit enters the battlefield from reinforcements. Stealthed units ignore the next enemy attack that would damage more than 1 target. Any damage will remove stealth.

entries.name: Urd Warrior
entries.title: Urd Warrior
entries.description: Urds are a primitive woodland species that has recently learned to craft tools. Elves consider Urds a nuisance, and sometimes set Urd traps around their settlements.

localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit.

localization.name: Time Bolt
localization.description: 300% damage to 1 enemy if they have 150TU or more in the turn order. 400% damage if the target has 200TU or more.

entries.name: Souldier
entries.title: Souldier
entries.description: When soldiers die at sea, the necrotic energies of the deep can bring their fish-eaten corpses back to fight new, unholy wars. For this reason, many cultures avoid naval battles.

localization.name: Brash Strike
localization.description: 135% damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit. Reflects 30% of the damage to the user.

localization.name: Life Flip
localization.description: Exchanges the user's current HP with the amount of HP they have currently lost. Usable once per battle.

entries.name: Nascent Hero
entries.title: Nascent Hero
entries.description: She's out to become the hero of her own story. But since she's not that skilled, she bands with others to learn their fighting techniques.

localization.name: Poison
localization.description: Grants the Poison status effect to 1 enemy. Allies gain 1 Spirit. This will overwrite other status effects on that unit.

localization.name: Poison Eater
localization.description: 400% damage to 1 enemy if they are poisoned.

entries.name: Bikki Novice
entries.title: Bikki Novice
entries.description: Bikki must prove themselves to their clans before coming of age. For many, this means finding honorable battles wherever they can. Thus, they're often on the road.

entries.name: Finn
entries.title: Finn
entries.description: Finn is a cheerful, friendly young man from the village of Rossé in the Rigland Central Plains. He loves his peaceful life in the countryside, but a powerful calling to help those in need, inspired by his mother's stories of the father he never knew, will lead him on a wild adventure far from the comfort of home.

entries.name: Krish
entries.title: Krish
entries.description: Krish are not normally aggressive. However, during the Pandemonium they get particularly defensive of their territories since monsters threaten their dens' safety. Trained Krish are common battle companions for young knights.

entries.name: Rizette
entries.title: Rizette
entries.description: Rizette is a young hunter from Westcarte, a village near Rossé in the Rigland Central Plains. Her unassuming demeanor hides an indomitable spirit and a loyal heart. A tragedy forces Rizette to flee her home with her younger brother Theo, of whom she is fiercely protective.

entries.name: Ludmilla
entries.title: Ludmilla
entries.description: Openhearted and an excellent leader, Captain Ludmilla commands the Third Regiment of the Rigland Red Dragon Knights. Her rigorous training routines that turn everything, including sleeping, into some form of training, are infamous among the Knights.

entries.name: Nagi
entries.title: Nagi
entries.description: A hopeless romantic with a silver tongue, Nagi is a bikki swordmaster who compares every woman he meets to a variety of flower. Don't mistake him for a simple dreamer though—Nagi is a man of action who fights with breathtaking agility and masterful swordsmanship.

entries.name: Astrid
entries.title: Astrid
entries.description: A proud perfectionist, Astrid is determined to live up to the expectations placed on her as daughter of the Commander of the Red Dragon Knights. She is as strict on others as she is on herself, but her air of confidence hides insecurities that she doesn't have what it takes to follow her father.

entries.name: Lightling
entries.title: Lightling
entries.description: Lightlings are highly sought after by many would-be monster trainers. Their winged adult forms can fly while carrying a passenger, making them excellent companions in battle. Because their sheer ferocity makes them nigh impossible to tame as adults, hunters often try to capture them while they are still infant Lightlings. However, this task is far from easy, as poisonous claws and a nasty bite make even Lightlings quite dangerous.

entries.name: Shockling
entries.title: Shockling
entries.description: While finding and capturing a rare Lightling is a test of skill for a monster trainer, taming the older Shockling is a test of patience. In addition to being much larger, Shocklings are also more aggressive and fearless, requiring constant supervision to keep them out of trouble. However, despite their disobedient tendencies, Shocklings care deeply for their masters, and they will protect them from any threats with zeal. They also like to bring their master fresh prey each morning, though this is not always quite as appreciated.

entries.name: Thundring
entries.title: Thundring
entries.description: Once a wild Shockling turns into a Thundring, it grows a pair of mighty wings and takes to the skies. From then on, it will spend the bulk of its time in flight. Wild Thundrings gather around storm clouds, absorbing their electrical energy. While hunting, a Thundring will swoop down and unleash this energy on unsuspecting prey, like a living lightning bolt. This is why the traditional Baltu'kud language uses the same word for both Thundrings and real lightning strikes.

entries.title: Flortuga
entries.description: Flortuga are popular companion monsters due to their charming appearance and their profound loyalty. If a Flortuga develops a fondness for someone, they will present them with a flower from their backs. A flower willingly given by a Flortuga will never wilt.

entries.title: Slizzle
entries.description: Slizzles slurm through the slippery slog of slathering slop of the Caves of Reia. Despite their animalistic appearance, they're rather intelligent, and have been found slurping and slathering little messages to one another. They also apparently communicate by intentionally manipulating their strange star patterns.

It's said that if one follows the star charts that surround their bodies, it'll lead to a great treasure. This is an odd rumor, since the stars move rapidly and don't seem to correspond to Erden's cosmology.

entries.description: After one ill-fated romance with a human prince and a sea witch's curse, the yanti'kud fell victim to one of history's worst propaganda campaigns, painting them as murderous seducers. The stereotype still persists.

entries.name: Needlepest
entries.title: Needlepest
entries.description: Most monster plants are simply annoying. Needlepests, possessed cacti, are downright deadly. They're not very robust, but they're difficult to engage in close combat.

localization.name: Poison
localization.description: Grants the Poison status effect to 2 enemies. Allies gain 1 Spirit. This will overwrite other status effects on those units.

localization.name: Push Back
localization.description: Pushes 1 enemy away from the battlefield to be next in line of the enemy reinforcements. This skill can only be used if there are 1 or more enemy reinforcements left.

localization.name: Auto Guard
localization.description: This unit is a Guardian. For any enemy attack that would hit 1 or 2 other allies, this unit has a 60% chance to redirect 1 hit to itself instead. This unit will not redirect skills that don't deal damage.

entries.name: Szi Pilgrim
entries.title: Szi Pilgrim
entries.description: Many szi tribes forage and spread when food runs short. Pilgrims scout for new homelands for their tribes, reporting back once they've found fertile ground.

localization.name: Attack
localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 2 Spirit.

localization.name: Purify
localization.description: Removes negative status effects like sleep, burn or poison from 1 ally.

localization.name: Quicken Ally
localization.description: 1 ally's TU costs for all of their skills are reduced by 20%. This will overwrite the effect of previous Quicken skills.

localization.name: Auto Guard
localization.description: This unit is a Guardian. For any enemy attack that would hit 1 or 2 other allies, this unit has a 60% chance to redirect 1 hit to itself instead. This unit will not redirect skills that don't deal damage.

entries.name: Wendigo
entries.title: Wendigo
entries.description: During the Pandemonium, great mountain apes froze to death, later breaking out of their icy shells imbued with cannibal spirits. Now, they feast on any bit of flesh they can find.

localization.name: Attack
localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit.

localization.name: Final Strike
localization.description: 400% damage to 1 enemy. Usable only if the user has no allies on the battlefield.

localization.name: Stun Revenge
localization.description: When this unit is defeated by an enemy, that enemy is stunned for 100TU.

entries.name: Elven Knight
entries.title: Elven Knight
entries.description: The elite knights of Elvenlent have mastered "bone weapons." Not actually made of bone, these ancient elven weapons are built hollow like a bird (and an elf's) bones in order to reduce weight for quicker strikes.

localization.name: Brash Strike
localization.description: 150% damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit. Reflects 30% of the damage to the user.

localization.name: Guard Breaker
localization.description: 400% damage to 1 enemy if they are a Guardian.

localization.name: Time Bolt
localization.description: 400% damage to 1 enemy if they have 150TU or more in the turn order. 500% damage if the target has 200TU or more.

localization.name: Spirit+ Revenge
localization.description: Whenever this unit is defeated by an enemy, allies gain 2 Spirit.

entries.name: Orc Shaman
entries.title: Orc Shaman
entries.description: Orcs maintain strong ties to nature. Each tribe trains a cadre of shamans who travel outside the tribe to understand other cultures. This can be complicated since they barely understand human tongues.

localization.name: Attack
localization.description: Damage to 1 enemy and allies gain 1 Spirit.

localization.name: Stealth Strike
localization.description: 400% damage to 1 enemy. Usable if the user has the Stealth status effect. This attack ignores any enemy status effects, damage reduction effects, revenge passive skills, and skin passive skills.

localization.name: Spirit- Revenge
localization.description: Whenever this unit is defeated by an enemy, the enemy party loses 2 Spirit.

entries.name: Erish
entries.title: Erish
entries.description: Erish are a strange hybrid of plant life and wildlife. When spiritual energy erupts in a place inhabited by both, sometimes they fuse to create a completely new, stronger creature. Erish are one of the more common examples of this magical phenomenon—a hybrid of a fox and ivy.

entries.name: Trissle
entries.title: Trissle
entries.description: Trissles are very common all over Erden. They're even popular pets in many cities, although this ends up making them something of a pest problem because they're very wild in adulthood and are often abandoned. So trissles are often found raiding urban garbage and demolishing gardens.

entries.name: Treasle
entries.title: Treasle
entries.description: One of the most important children's stories in Erden, the Treasle and the Troubadour, put Treasles forever in the cultural eye. In the story, a troubadour lost her hand in war, and couldn't play the lute. After falling to depression, a whimsical Treasle showed her other ways she could tell her stories to the world.

entries.name: Pike Apostle
entries.title: Pike Apostle
entries.description: The lesser-ranking members of the Pike Paladins are determined to prove themselves to the rest of their order. They will accept any challenge in the hopes of earning the privilege of wielding a spear, the holiest of weapons. Some set out on pilgramges across the land, vanquishing evildoers and dedicating their victories to their idol, the legendary Vensha Dayne.

entries.name: Pike Apostle
entries.title: Pike Apostle
entries.description:
Different factions of the Pike Paladins exist, and they often disagree about certain aspects of Vensha Dayne's lore. They meet regularly to debate and share ideas.

Some believe Vensha defeated an evil aquatic demon by having a yanti witch cast a spell on her so she could breathe underwater. Others believe this battle took place on land, and it was the demon who was able to breathe air by tricking a yanti witch into assisting her.

entries.name: Cryosaur
entries.title: Cryosaur
entries.description: When the Pandemonium hits, spiritual energy finds its way into numerous objects. Sometimes, this means those objects become animate. And in the Silverdrake Mountains, this sometimes means fossils of long-extinct dinosaurs. Cryosaurs are these fossils, resurrected by the magic of the cold.

entries.description: An apocryphal tale say a Kadu was once confronted after eating a village. Seeing that the Kadu seemed somewhat remorseful, a clever young man proposed a deal with her—if she protected their land from invaders, she could eat the invaders, along with a certain amount of the village's food supply. This worked well for both parties, until the Kadu decided she missed the taste of village.

entries.name: Vivica
entries.title: The Radiant Gladiator
entries.description:
The youngest of a family of distinguished warriors, Vivica knew she had to do something special to get out from the shadows of her seven older brothers. When she was offered her first, child-sized sword, she wrinkled her nose and grabbed the largest greatsword she could lift.

Wielding the blade was gruelling work, but the discouragement of her family members only fuelled Vivica's fire. To their amazement, she could soon wield a blade as tall as she was as with unnatural speed.

entries.name: Vivica

entries.description:
Vivica quickly made a name for herself in local tournaments, defeating opponents twice her size.

Impressed by her power and dedication, Vivica's father offered to make her the next family head, much to her brothers' dismay. However, Vivica refused, and instead set out on a journey across Westland, determined to make her mark on the world without relying on her family name.

entries.name: Trevyn
entries.title: The Petal Paladin
entries.description:
Trevyn was always a talented swordsman, a quality that made him overconfident and brash in his youth. His arrogance got him into a duel with a blind human swordmaster, but he had his world turned upside down when the elderly woman defeated him.

Believing that she had the key to something he lacked, Trevyn pleaded with her to take him on as her disciple. At first the woman would have none of it, but Trevyn's newfound humility and enthusiasm eventually won her over. From that day forth, Trevyn trained to use his sword without his eyesight.

entries.name: Trevyn
entries.title: The Petal Paladin
entries.description:
Under his new master's tutelage, Trevyn quickly became adept at using his hearing to compensate for his sight. Soon he could hear a single flower petal fluttering on the wind, and then slice it cleanly into half.

But his teacher's old age soon caught up with her. On her deathbed, she confided in Trevyn that she feared her life was meaningless, as whatever she had accomplished, a swordmaster with sight could have easily done better. Trevyn assured her that her this was not the case, and swore to prove that her techniques were unrivalled. He now travels through Erden to hone his skills and fulfill his promise.

entries.name: Kavaat Merc
entries.title: Infernal Mercenary
entries.description: The resemblance between sziviik and the Kavaat has led some scholars to suggest they may share a common ancestor. The fact that both groups have given the world many powerful warriors supports this theory. Unlike the sziviik, however, the Kavaat have no particular homeland, and tend to operate alone.

entries.name: Dargarossa
entries.title: Bane of the Ashiran Sea
entries.description: Few names inspire as much fear in sailors as "Dargarossa," the most notorious pirate to ever cruise the Ashiran Sea. With his ferocious crew in tow, Dargarossa has plundered countless ships and sunk just as many. His favorite prey are ships carrying high-class passengers, as he has an insatiable appetite for the valuable trinkets they tend to wear. As such, the nobility living along the coast have placed an exuberant reward on his squid-like head. But the local bounty hunters don't dare pursue him, as all those who have tried in the past now rest at the bottom of the sea.

entries.name: Dargarossa
entries.title: Bane of the Ashiran Sea
entries.description: Some say that he was formerly a normal man, cursed by the goddess of the sea for rejecting her affections. Others say he is a demon of the deep, drawn to the surface by the promise of treasure. Whatever his true nature may be, it is clear that Dargarossa has no intention of divulging it to anyone. His crew know better than to ask him about it, for those who do are sent "fishing"—they have one ankle tied by rope to the aft and are then tossed overboard, while the ship cruises at full speed.

entries.name: Miriel
entries.title: High Angel of the Morning
entries.description: Parents all across Westland tuck their children into bed with tales of guardian angels watching over their slumber. As a soldier of the Angelic Order of the Morning Light, it is Mirel's duty to protect children from ghoulish demons that prey upon their dreams. Each night, she and her comrades fight from dusk until the first rays of the morning sun.

entries.name: Miriel
entries.title: High Angel of the Morning
entries.description:
The Sky of Omens—a supernatural event of unknown origin that occurred in the skies of Erden two millennia ago. The sun vanished, and the heavens blazed in the glow of a many-colored aurora.

Without the light of morning to chase away the demons of the dusk, Mirel and her comrades found themselves unable to rest. When the soldiers around her grew hopeless, believing the dark would never end, it was Miriel who urged them on, and when the sun finally returned, she was awarded the coveted title of High Angel for her bravery.

entries.name: Ja'Nafaat
entries.title: Hero of Lu'Ganan
entries.description:
Before the cycle of the Pademonium began on Erden, the ancient szi had developed an advanced civilization in the Habsu Desert.

Ja'Nafaat was the head of the temple guards of Lu'Ganan, entrusted with the protection of the faithful and their place of worship. Though he and the other guards were all well-trained, they never expected deal with anything worse than the occasional rowdy visitor.

That all changed with the onset of the first Pandemonium.

entries.name: Ja'Nafaat
entries.title: Hero of Lu'Ganan
entries.description:
The invading hordes quickly devastated the szi civilization, and the survivors flocked to the temple of Lu'Ganan in hopes that their gods would provide them protection. Suddenly burdened with the task of defending these refugees from a bloodthirsty foe unlike anything they had encountered before, Ja'Nafaat felt overwhelmed.

Yet he refused to let his people and gods down. With uncharacteristic ferocity, he led the temple guards to successfully drive the enemy back after a six-day siege. The grateful refugees immortalized his name as one of the greatest warriors in szi history.

entries.name: Jedariel
entries.title: Otherworldly War Machine
entries.description:
When did the war begin? All who knew the answer have long since perished. It is a war for the sake of waging war.

In Jedariel's world, never-ending strife is the natural state of things. They are one of many soldiers, each of them perfect weapons of war. Their only goal is to take out as many enemies as possible, before their durable bodies finally fail them.

entries.name: Jedariel
entries.title: Otherworldly War Machine
entries.description:
Even in a world of perfect war machines, Jedariel stood out from the rest. They could single-handedly take down dozens of other enemies, so any battle they participated in quickly became one-sided. 

It was only natural that eventually, they would draw the attention of one of the higher powers of their world. In exchange for Jedariel's utter servitude, this entity offered them even greater strength and the opportunity to use it in worlds beyond their own. Jedariel wasted no time in accepting this bargain, for there is nothing they desire more than greater power and further strife.

Now, Jedariel eagerly goes forth to defeat their master's enemies, or to die trying.

entries.name: Shanna
entries.title: Cat-Eared Mercenary
entries.description:
Found abandoned as an infant near a sarru settlement, Shanna is a human child raised by sarru foster parents. The settlement treated her as one of their own, and Shanna grew up with very little interest in her human origins. She has even gone so far as to fashion a hood with cat-like ears and braid her long hair into a tail in order to make herself look more sarru. 

Obeying sarru tradition, Shanna has just begun her five-year journey to hone her combat skills as a mercenary. She hasn't had much success so far, as her shy demeanor makes her seem ill-suited for the job.

entries.name: Shanna
entries.title: Cat-Eared Mercenary
entries.description:
However, nothing could be further from the truth. Shanna has always had incredible natural talent as a fighter, able to swing around giant mauls with surprising ease despite her small stature. 

The sarru of her settlement wonder whether one or both of her birth parents might have been great warriors. Shanna may learn something about them in her travels over the next five years, but for the moment, she is perfectly content for her true parentage to remain a mystery.

entries.name: Velle
entries.title: Elven Bard
entries.description:
As Velle grows older, she wonders what will become of her own life after she passes away. Who will remember her? Who will speak of her own story?

Velle's bardic tradition forbids one from telling any tale of their own life. But nevertheless, she hopes that someday someone will craft a story about her and preserve her memory for the future, just as she is doing for tales of the past.

entries.name: En Soldier
entries.title: Principality Commander
entries.description: The En are an order of Principality. Principalities watch over the peoples, protecting them from evil. They will protect that which they deem good at all costs, but they see only good and evil—no inbetween. For the people of Erden, this has made them a terrifying force, as many of their cultural icons have performed deeds that a Principality could perceive as evil.

entries.name: Zeregia
entries.title: Szi Beast of Reckoning
entries.description: The sziviik developed complex aqueduct systems early in their civilization. However, they halted progress for fear that any massive body of water would invite the Zeregia, and kept irrigation and water supplies minimal. When outsiders scoff, the sziviik simply say, "We'll see who is the last standing when the Zeregia come."

entries.name: Jin
entries.title: Bikki Wanderer
entries.description:
When the bikki came to Westland, they left behind much of their traditional magic in Eastland. Ambitious bikki spellcasters would attempt to make the journey back to their ancestor's homelands, in hopes of learning their forgotten secrets. The passage was long and filled with peril, and many of these pilgrims were never heard from again.

When the aspiring mage Jin decided to make the trip, his friends and family were deeply concerned. Jin's sense of direction was so terrible, it was not uncommon for him to get lost on the way to his own house. Nevertheless, Jin was too stubborn to be persuaded otherwise, and he departed for Eastland.

entries.name: Jin
entries.title: Bikki Wanderer
entries.description:
Jin was not heard from again for over a decade, and those who knew him began to assume the worst. However, one day he suddenly returned, looking none worse for wear and adorned with fancy robes.

He had indeed reached his destination of Eastland, but not before some lengthy detours through both the northern and southern continents of Erden. As a result, he has learned far more than he would have by only visiting Eastland.

With his expanded knowledge, Jin began his own school of bikki magic, and now aspiring bikki mages seek wisdom there rather than attempt the arduous journey themselves.

entries.name: Jayle
entries.title: Horned God of Festivity
entries.description: Jayle started as a god of festivity, living his life among the revelers and celebrants. He appeared at one party then the next, feasting on the sense of celebration and joyous abandon. He fed on the beauty of the moment, the excitement and uncertainty of superlatively salacious gatherings. And after the last Pandemonium, he fed well.

entries.name: Jayle
entries.title: Horned God of Festivity
entries.description: Surrounded by friends, Jayle eventually learned the most tragic lesson—nothing is forever. Fragile, mortal lives can be ended, and he lives on forever. When he lost one of his friends at the claws of a monster, he stopped feeding on friendship, and began feeding on the unbridled bounty of vengeance. He would punish those who destroy friendships.

entries.title: Goddess of Plenty
entries.description: Inanna is a goddess of abundance and renewal. However, her expression of this is unique among gods. Inanna falls out of favor with worshipers every few centuries. entries.name: Inanna

entries.name: Kemek
entries.title: Soul of Retribution
entries.description: To call a Kemek, the elves say, one must draw a circle in their own blood. They sit inside the circle with a gift and state their grievance over and over until the Kemek appears. The Kemek looks over the gift, and judges its worth in respect to the justice expected of them. If they accept, they bring justice. If they refuse, they instead slay the summoner.

entries.name: Beryx
entries.title: Jewel of the Forest
entries.description:
Beryxes are elusive creatures said to live in the deepest regions of a forest. Most people know them only through a series of popular folk tales that feature Beryxes as tricksters, who fool greedy or arrogant individuals into humiliating themselves.

These tales have some basis in reality, as Beryxes are known to playfully harass travelers who become lost in the forest. In the process however, the traveler always winds up back on the beaten path again.

entries.name: Beryx
entries.title: Jewel of the Forest
entries.description:
A mature Beryx develops beautiful crystals on its body. These crystals are thought to have mystical properties. Every now and then, a foolish individual will attempt to capture a Beryx, in order to harvest these crystals for a quick profit. 

They soon discover that despite the Beryx's playful reputation, they quickly become a terrible force of nature when threatened. Beryxes will fearlessly and savagely fight anything that threatens them, even if the attacker is several times larger in size. As such, even the largest predators in the forest will step aside if they encounter one.

entries.description: Akatsuki justifies his behavior as "good" because while he'll challenge anyone who might best him, he argues that most of those candidates are inherently evil. So, in his quest to be the best, he'll vanquish mostly evil forces.

entries.name: Imran
entries.title: The Walking Storm
entries.description:
Though most people value harmony over strife, Imran was always the opposite. Even as a child, he was always caught up in some kind of fight. As soon as he was old enough to participate in wars against neighboring clans, it was clear that Imran was to become the greatest warrior of the Dust Steppes.

However, he soon tired of defeating fellow humans, so he turned to hunting down vicious monsters for a greater challenge. One day, he tracked down the whereabouts of a legendary dragon, but even he was no match against it. However, the dragon found a kindred spirit in the ever battle hungry Imran, and they agreed to have a rematch some day.

entries.name: Imran
entries.title: The Walking Storm
entries.description:
As a sign of this promise, the dragon granted Imran small portion of its power. As a result of this boon, Imran's body now constantly crackles with electricity. Any warrior lucky enough to land a blow against him will find themselves electrocuted.

Combined with his combat prowess, Imran had now become nigh unstoppable on the battlefield. Yet he knows that he must become even stronger, in preparation for when he would meet the dragon again. For this time, the battle will undoubtedly end with one of them dying.

With a greater zeal than ever before, Imran rushes forward into conflict, striving to ready himself as much as possible before the fateful day.

entries.name: Vesh
entries.title: Will of the Soil
entries.description: After bearing the wanton destruction wrought by the forces of the Eternal for several cycles, the soils of Erden stirred. Determined to rid themselves of the disease known as the Pandemonium, they created Vesh. Born from the earth itself, Vesh was brought forth in the height of an endless trial. It knows nothing of itself, and even the planet that bore it knows not its future.

entries.name: Vesh
entries.title: Will of the Soil
entries.description: From the moment it was created, Vesh has continued an endless quest to define itself. Through encounters both friendly and hostile it developed a personality, and arrived at a personal philosophy of sorts. It began its journey without words, but through interaction with the inhabitants of Erden, it taught itself to speak. Though it makes no effort to explain its actions, many have been saved by the swiftness of its spear.

entries.name: Grenzor
entries.title: Scourge of the Hinterlands
entries.description: Northerners warn travelers about the Grenzor, ancient beasts that must keep a fire burning deep in their hearts. They do so by devouring the warm, still beating hearts of others—the bigger the better. Though they prefer larger prey, when this becomes scarce, no living creatures, including people, are safe from them.

As such, very little else is known about these predators, though they are commonly referenced in folklore of the north, including many stories to discourage children from misbehaving. When someone goes missing there, the locals automatically assume that they have fallen prey to a ravenous Grenzor.

entries.name: Vonn
entries.title: Bearer of the Demon Maul
entries.description:
Vonn was a child of a noble elven family who was entrusted with defending their region during a prior Pandemonium.

One day, in a sealed vault deep within his father's castle, Vonn found a giant maul that had become a prison for a powerful demon lord named Drakkus. The demon promised great power to any that would wield this maul in battle, and eager to aid his father against the forces of the Pandemonium, Vonn foolishly carried the weapon out of the vault.

Just as the demon had promised, Vonn became nearly invincible in battle. Despite his father's grave concerns about the weapon, the elven forces soon came to rely on the young hero in the struggle against the Pandemonium.

entries.name: Vonn
entries.title: Bearer of the Demon Maul
entries.description:
But Vonn's heroics soon came to an end. One stormy night, the forces of the Pandemonium laid siege to Vonn's home itself. Vonn eagerly joined the fray against them with his maul, and it didn't take long for him to crush all of the attacking forces.

At that moment, the demon in the maul intentionally loosened his grip on the boy's mind, so that he could see what had really transpired. There was no siege, there were no Aeon Knights... Only his beloved family, who now lay dead and broken throughout the castle.

As the truth dawned on Vonn, his mind shattered, allowing the demon to fully claim his soul.

entries.name: Kirin
entries.title: Mythweaver
entries.description:
The illusive Kirin are common characters in Erden mythology, but their significance greatly differs from culture to culture. The Zuo people of Eastland believe that the appearance of a Kirin is a sign that a god is near, whilst the legends of the Akashi people depict the Kirin as a symbol of great happiness.

Few have ever met a Kirin, and reports of their nature vary from gentle and majestic to divinely terrifying.

entries.name: Kirin
entries.title: Mythweaver
entries.description: An ancient folktale found among various cultural groups in Westland tells the story of a child abandoned at birth and raised by a Kirin. The child, in some tales called Enkidu and by others Eabani, grew up in the spirit world compassionate and gentle. When she turned 16, the Kirin took her back to the material realm, where she was greatly saddened by the division between its peoples. She begged her foster parent to do something, and moved by his ward's conviction, the Kirin led a procession of his breathren to thunder across the night sky.

entries.name: Kaidaros
entries.title: Riptide Guardian
entries.description:
When the first Dragon of Water was slain by the Eternal, he gifted Kaidaros his power, and she used it well. It was not she who brought warfare and corruption to the sea. It was the humans, and their greed.

Their crafts started plain, barely anything more than hollow logs on the water. But as they grew ever more prideful and sailed ever further, they slew one another on her waters, hunted her ocean's bounty until entire species died. She could allow it no longer.

Her waves ripped mast from hull and dragged the villainous men to her depths. For generations, she pushed them back to their shores, until no one dared venture forth into the sea.

No one, that is, but the people who looked at her waters with awe, not avarice.

entries.name: Kaidaros
entries.title: Riptide Guardian
entries.description:
On Erden's widest sea, there was an island laden with wild fruit, fresh water, and good soil. Kaidaros often surfaced there when she tired of the ocean's depths, and she cared for it deeply. When she saw a ship full of humans sailing toward its shores one day, she lashed out with all her fury, desperate to keep it safe from their bloodstained hands.

But these men were not like the others. As the waves rose around them, they offered up a song of reverence to her and her domain. She had no reason to trust them. Men had begged her for their lives before...but never with such sincerity. It shook her to her core.

She could not destroy any creature that came to her in peace.

entries.title: Ga'Luga
entries.description:
The son of the chief of Me'Rizanté and also its strongest warrior, Ga'Luga is honorable, brave, and proud. Though he is suspicious of outsiders, if you win his trust you will struggle to find a more fiercely loyal comrade.

However, his belief that all things are solvable with brute force may land you in some sticky situations.

entries.name: Mysha
entries.title: Mysha
entries.description:
A girl from Urg Roa who lives with her grandfather, Gilgan. Bright and cheerful, and never seen without a smile. Mysha grew up admiring her grandfather and uncle, both renowned swordmasters, and idolizes Druke especially. From a young age, Mysha has trained with a sword alongside her childhood friend Ga'Luga.
Since she's not kud, Mysha grew up training alongside people much bigger and stronger than her. Still, her innate speed and agility, and her unflagging hard work, are the source of her strength.

entries.name: Rizette
entries.title: The Cerulean Crestbearer
entries.description:
"Are you sure that's not too heavy for you, Theo?"

"I'm fine, Riz. I can carry this much."

"It feels like only yesterday you were smaller than that bag."

"You're treating me like a kid again... You're not that much older than me, you know."

"That doesn't matter. You're my responsibility since Mom and Dad aren't here anymore."

"Riz... Look at the village!"

"!?"

"Those are flames!"

entries.name: Rizette
entries.title: The Cerulean Crestbearer
entries.description:
"Riz? Are you sure you're okay?"

"What do you mean?"

"With this Cerulean Crest thing... Everything happened so suddenly. How do you feel about all of it?"

"Theo... You're sweet. But I'm okay, really. I wanted the power to fight, and now I have it. And besides..."

"Besides?"

"Now Finn doesn't have to bear everything alone. He put his life on the line to help us when he didn't even know us, and he ended up with the responsibility of the Crimson Crest. If there's something I can do to lessen his burden, I'll do it."

"Riz..."

entries.name: Finn
entries.title: The Crimson Crestbearer
entries.description:
"Man, Finn sure is excited about this whole Lightling thing..."

"You guys're headed out again today?"

"Yep... Can't fight what's in your blood, I s'pose."

"Me missus' been saying he's looking more like his Pa every day."

"C'mon Elran, let's go!"

"Speak of the devil. I don't know how you keep up."

"I better get moving before he explodes. Keep this place safe while we're gone, boys!"

entries.name: Finn
entries.title: The Crimson Crestbearer
entries.description:
"Hey, Callen? Just in case I don't make it through this—"

"That's not even in the realm of possibility."

"If I... If I become something that's not me anymore—"

"That's not going to happen!"

"But if it does, could you give this letter to Rizette for me?"

"I won't. I refuse to deliver another letter like that."

"Another one? Callen, were you the one who—"

"Fine, I'll keep this letter for you. But when we emerge victorious, you're going to be so embarrassed, Finn!"

"...Thank you, Callen. I'm sorry."

"Honestly! You and that idiot father of yours are two peas in a pod."

entries.name: Orzachron
entries.title: Organica War Machine MK
entries.description:
This mysterious beast resembles in form a dragon, yet it gives off no scent and hunts for no food. The weaponized attachments affixed to its body give the impression it has been artificially modified, but it bears no traces of the magical irradiation signature in other golems.

Fiercely territorial, the Orzachron utilize their mysterious and destructive weaponry to assault any who draw close.

entries.name: Baltruk
entries.title: Lady Luck's Toy
entries.description:
Good Luck Baltruk.
His village razed by an iron dragon, 
the hamlet he hid in destroyed by monsters, 
the merchants who rescued him prey for tiger hornets, 
the port town where he washed ashore raided by orcs
and the village that sheltered him crushed by the Aeon Legion. 

That, is good luck Baltruk! 
This most fortunate of men entered the trials of the knighthood, 
and why did he triumph? 
Because he is good luck Baltruk, plaything of lady luck!
-From a master minstrel's song

entries.name: Baltruk
entries.title: Lady Luck's Toy
entries.description:
"I thought I was just unlucky, but I was wrong. In the height of the Pandemonium, I saw countless people suffering as I once did. Feeling the same rage and sorrow I once did.

I have certainly lived through calamity, but what's key is that I lived! Now I have the chance to reach out to those in pain, to lend them what power I have and inspire them to face their misfortunes without fear!"

Whether in dreams or reality she is always surrounded by a forcefield called "My Little Garden", and she can make this into many forms that she can use to fight. entries.title: Goddess of Bounty
entries.description: The Great Sage of Kisenkyo, born to a great water dragon and a human mother. Extremely skilled in onmyodo, she protects the swamplands from any who would harm its inhabitants. Her magic places intense strain on her body, and she spends most of her time resting in her pocket dimension, the Realm of Reia, or studying her magic. As a result, her social skills are somewhat atrophied.

Oh, my Lady, in the face of your brilliance I am but a love-stricken shadow. O radiance, o poetry given form! Allow this unworthy one to compare thee to a flower..." —From \'The Love Songs of Nagi\', Vol. 3, page 12

entries.name: Artimeia
entries.title: Lady of the White Moon
entries.description:
Many old legends speak of the Lady of the White Moon, an ancient being who slumbers in a vast palace on one of Erden's moons.

It is said that if one were to visit her there, she would rise from her sleep and offer the visitor a prophecy of their future. Most of Erden's mythical heroes are said to have received advice from her, aiding them in fulfilling their grand destinies.

However, these legends offer no hints as to how to get to her palace in the first place. Various plans have been devised, such as building some kind of flying machine or creating a powerful teleportation spell, but only a handful of eccentrics have actually attempted them. Most become discouraged by recurring dreams of their plans failing, often in an extremely spectacular fashion.

entries.name: Artimeia
entries.title: Lady of the White Moon
entries.description:
When Artimeia sleeps, she dreams of the future. But because the future is a tangled web of various possibilities, choices, and fates, Artimeia must sleep for ages at a time to properly understand how her actions might change outcomes. 

Thus, she tries to minimize her role as much as possible in the events that unfold in Erden, taking action only if it's absolutely necessary to ensure the world's survival. This usually amounts to only granting grains of advice to the right people at the right time. However, she understands that dire times call for more dire measures, and she will occasionally join a potential hero to help them fulfill their destiny.

entries.name: Le Fay
entries.title: The Witch of Avalon
entries.description: Le Fay is the leader of the nine spirits who reside in the distant isle of Avalon. She guards the Fruit of Wisdom, which bestows great knowledge to  whoever should consume it. It is said that she grants the fruit only to those who pass a series of trials. She is a cynic with a fondness for mischief who speaks in half-truths and riddles. This is part of the trials as well, as her persona helps Le Fay ascertain the true nature of those seeking the fruit she guards.

entries.name: Le Fay
entries.title: The Witch of Avalon
entries.description:
Le Fay can show one who has consumed the Fruit of Wisdom what glories their future holds. However, for most this path is paved with slaughter and ends in tyranny. Once the choice is made to consume the fruit, there is no turning back.

Le Fay well knows the ultimate fate of those who choose to do so. No matter what they decide to do with their gained wisdom, they will seek to return to Avalon once more at the end of their days.

entries.name: Astrid
entries.title: Hero of the West
entries.description:
"First Raid Unit. Tasks: curry favor with the Serpent Princess."
"No way they'll be assigned to the front line."
"If I could, I'd stay with this unit even when the Pandemonium is over."
"They're really just toy soldiers."
"Listen, in our case-."
"What are you doing over there
Did you not hear my summons!?"
"Apologies ma'am! We'll be there right away!"

entries.name: Astrid
entries.title: Hero of the West
entries.description:
"Your mother led battalions, Tori?"
"I heard that she fought by my father's side in the last Pandemonium."
"According to the records, they were married soon after it finished. Once you were born it seems she lead a squad against the remaining demons, but went missing. The records are short on details."
"Perhaps there was something they could not write?"
"Have you asked your father?"
"No, and I shan't. When he is ready, he will tell me."
"As you wish."

entries.name: Oumei
entries.title: Spirit of Spring
entries.description:
I have heard many outlandish tales in my travels. In the Majas Desert, for example, the locals tell of a hidden grove where grows a tree that blooms only once every twenty years. What's more, the spirit of this tree is said to be a woman of surpassing beauty who will grant any wish made to her. A fabrication, to be sure, but a fascinating one.
(From the Chronicles of Erden, Eastland Chapter)

entries.name: Rashanar
entries.title: The Burning Hunger
entries.description: The Rashanar, unlike other dragons of Erden, cannot reproduce. Rahaneer are the result of a single prehistoric event. Most fell into slumber upon this strange, magical birth, but some have awakened at separate times across the millenia. While individual Rashanar have personalities, every single one shares a hunger for fire and a want for devastation.

entries.name: Alba
entries.title: Unyielding Will
entries.description: The first, and only, thing to be said about Scales is how stubborn she is. She has no concept of compromise. Once she's decided on a course of action, no force on Erden can stop her, and of course she won't hear a word of advice or caution either. She didn't even consult with any of her fellow villagers before inviting herself along with me. What bad luck, to be stuck with such a hard-headed partner.

entries.title: Unyielding Will
entries.description: The only other thing of note about my partner is her knowledge of herbal medicine. Though limited, it did save my life once. I considered my debt repaid when I gave up my wish, but she now believes she owes me a debt instead, and has invited herself along on my journey to repay it. Perhaps we'll find a cure for stubborn foolishness along the way.

entries.title: Vengeful Wanderer
entries.description: The big thing about this fellow is his sharp tongue. At least he doesn't discriminate. Sziviik, kud, humans, even elves — he hates everyone equally. Every time he opens his mouth, it's insult after insult after insult. But...from what I hear, he wasn't always like this. Some things can change a man.

entries.name: Merdain
entries.title: Knight of Vengeance
entries.description:
A former knight of Hymelia, and member of the lowest rank of nobility. His father Artorias began his career as a common footsoldier, but his skills saw him promoted to General and earned him land and a noble title. Having the greatest admiration for his father, Merdain followed in his footsteps, and he quickly became a very promising knight, despite the derision he endured for his low rank and common origins.
However, the nobles of the Senate conspired against Artorias, and he fell on the battlefield, leaving Merdain torn between his loyalty and vengeance.

localization.description: This unit gains the Armor status effect after dealing damage with an attack. This will overwrite other status effects on the unit. This will not activate if the unit begins its turn with armor. Armor grants 50% damage reduction from the next attack, then the effect is removed.

entries.name: Merdain
entries.title: Knight of Vengeance
entries.description:
On Avalon, Merdain learned that his father took advantage of an unnatural power, the Fruit of Wisdom. The realization that even his father, with all his resourcefulness, loyalty, and hard work, had to resort to supernatural power to be acknowledged by the nobility of Hymelia sank Merdain into a deep despair.
What good is loyalty to a country that despises you?

entries.name: Lucius
entries.title: Fighter Against Fate
entries.description:
A knight of Hymelia. Unlike his childhood friend Merdain, he comes from a high-ranking, long-standing noble house. He was sickly and weak as a child, but he forced himself to keep up with his friends Merdain and Maxima, overcame his weakness, and is now an outstanding knight.
Lucius strongly respected Merdain's father Artorias, who rose from the rank of footsoldier to the rank of General, and then to nobility. He sees Artorias as proof that anything is possible if you work hard, and believes he can overcome his illness too if he only works hard enough.

localization.name: Spear Mastery
localization.description: Can use weapon skills of Spears in addition to their original preferred weapon type.

entries.name: Lucius
entries.title: Fighter Against Fate
entries.description:
Maxima: "First one to the big oak is the winner, okay? Ready, set, go! ...Haha, I win again! You're so slow, Lucius!"

Merdain: "Lay off, Maxima. Last month he couldn't even keep up with us, remember?"

Maxima: "Hmph. You always take Lucius's side."

Lucius: "Sorry, I just... need to catch my breath. Can we rest for a minute?"

"Merdain: Knights don't get to rest on the battlefield! Come on, get up! We're doing best of three!"

Counter stance cannot be gained if the user already has the Counter Stance status effect or has gotten the current turn from Counter Stance.

entries.name: Velsa
entries.title: Bearer of Corpses
entries.description:
One of the Oracles, the nine spirits who protect and oversee Avalon. Her main job is to retrieve the corpses of those who return to Avalon, and unlike her elder sister Le Fay, she doesn't often interact with living humans.
She cares the most about keeping the peace on the island, and if outside invaders ever arrive on Avalon, she's the first one to engage them in battle. Despite her slender build, she is monstrously strong. Generally speaking, the other eight Oracles never even have a chance to fight before the battle is over.

entries.name: Velsa
entries.title: Bearer of Corpses
entries.description:
"What, pray tell, is he doing?"
"He says he is building a raft. It is amusing, watching him struggle with the trees and vines."
"Amusing enough to watch for a whole afternoon? He rejected the Fruit. He's unworthy, and you're wasting your time."
"Really? Perhaps we are mistaken about that. Perhaps I should observe him further to be sure, even across the ocean..."
"...Le Fay, you..."
"Be at ease, dear sister. It was only a joke."
"...If you're sure."

entries.name: Esma
entries.title: Bride of the Sea
entries.description: The young Queen of the island nation of Laomis, and head priestess of Nautica, the Primordial of Water. The people of Laomis depend entirely on the sea for their lives, so they view Esma, whose job it is to keep the favor of the tempestuous Primordial, with the utmost respect. Though she's a fine ruler and well-liked by her people, she's still very young, and sometimes feels like she'll buckle under the weight of her responsibilities.

entries.name: Esma
entries.title: Bride of the Sea
entries.description:
Though a demure and gentle ruler now, Esma used to be quite a tomboy, sneaking out of the palace to go down to the docks, listen to the pirates' tales of their exploits, and dream of the world on the other side of the ocean.
\Over the course of her visits, she fell in love with a dashing young pirate; but he refused her feelings. Heartbroken, in a moment of weakness, she prayed to Nautica for revenge... and upon hearing that his ship had been lost to the sea, she realized that she had done something unforgivable. The regret still torments her, but the harsh lesson helped shape her into the responsible monarch she is today.

entries.name: Astrid
entries.title: Hero of Rigland (On Break)
entries.description: "Ludmilla was very insistent I come here, but what exactly does one do on "vacation"? There's nothing here but sand and seawater. The sun is too bright, the sand is too hot, and... I can't believe I'm dressed like this outside my bedchamber. And yet... somehow, I feel..."

entries.name: Astrid
entries.title: Hero of Rigland (On Break)
entries.description:
Mysha: I wonder how Astrid's doing.
Theo: She doesn't seem like the vacation type, does she?
Rizette: Don't worry, I'm sure she's having a great time! I wish I could have gone, too...
Mysha: Once things settle down, why don't we all go to the beach together?
Nagi: Yes! A thousand times, yes! By all means, let us all go together!
Rizette: ...I'll go if Nagi stays here.
Nagi: Wha... h-have a heaaart! Waaaah!
Theo: He's bawling?!

entries.name: Ludmilla
entries.title: Summertime Valkyrie
entries.description:
"...Marshal? What are you doing now?"
"Ah, Lady Astrid! This is a sport known as 'surfing'. You stand on this board-like thing, and use it to ride the waves!"
"You certainly seem to be enjoying yourself."
"Yes, of course! Would you like to join me? It makes for a fine way to train your balance!"
"I-I'll pass, thank you."
(The Marshal doesn't seem to draw a line between training and recreation...)

This effect will go away only if this unit redirects an attack, targeting this unit directly will not remove it. This unit cannot have the Counter Stance status effect while it has guard once.

entries.name: Ludmilla
entries.title: Summertime Valkyrie
entries.description:
Laomis: the glimmering pearl of the South Seas. Every Riglander dreams of visiting this tropical paradise at least once, and the Red Dragon Knights' hard-headed Marshal is no exception. However, with all of her responsibilities, it seemed like a faraway dream within a dream. She had halfway given up when Queen Esma's invitation descended like a gift from on high.
"I cannot afford to fail on this mission. I must enjoy myself to the fullest...!"
Vacation is no excuse to slack off, after all.

entries.name: Adena
entries.title: Blue Hurricane
entries.description:
Despite her young age, Adena is the first mate and right hand of Dargarossa, the fearsome Bane of the Ashiran Sea. While on her way to be sold as a slave in a far-off land, her ship was raided by Dargarossa and she was freed; with nowhere else to go, she decided to stick around, and became a pirate. Though free-spirited, she respects Dargarossa deeply.
When the call to board is sounded and the battle is joined, she's the first one across the gap, leaping to the deck of the other ship and laying waste around her with her spear. Despite her young age, the crew respects her second only to the Captain himself, and they call her "Sis".

entries.name: Adena
entries.title: Blue Hurricane
entries.description:
Cap'n, it weren't me what hit you with the ball! It was Sis! C'mon, Sis, back me— huh?! Where'd she go?!
"......"
"C'mon, Cap'n, calm down! Huh? Yer gettin' a bump on yer head? Nah, that's just what yer head always looks—Aaaaugh!"
Splash!
"Whew, he went far! Might be a new record. Ah, hey, land ho! Someone haul that sorry carcass outta the drink and make ready for landfall!"

entries.name: Maxima
entries.title: Fiery Princess
entries.description:
The eldest daughter of the Hymelian royal family. Though a princess, she is strong-willed and a highly skilled warrior, so she often fights on the front lines. The pride of the Hymelian Knights, Merdain, can just about hold his own with her. Their other childhood friend, Lucius, has practically never beaten her in any contest.
The nobility and conservative voices in government disapprove of her un-princesslike behavior, at least behind closed doors, but soldiers and the common people adore her.
In every generation, at least one member of the Hymelian royal family has a contract with the primeval fire spirit Volcana. Currently, that responsibility falls to Maxima. Volcana has a particular soft spot for the gallant and brave Maxima, and Maxima views the flame spirit less as an object of worship, and more as a sometimes harsh, but loving mother.

entries.name: Maxima
entries.title: Fiery Princess
entries.description:
Maxima was quite a tomboy in her youth, and she would sneak out of the palace to play with her friends Merdain and Lucius. She was repeatedly told not to associate with Merdain because of his low standing, but rather than obey, she wondered why people had to be so cruel to one of her best friends.
Spending time outside the palace, Maxima had many chances to interact with common-born soldiers and smallfolk, and she began to question the nobility's focus on bloodline. Why should the house someone is born into dictate their worth in life? Eventually, she realized that she wanted to change her country.

entries.name: Elmina
entries.title: Void Renegade
entries.description:
Every thief comes with her own sad story, but Elmina's is especially heinous. Living as a street urchin in Hymelia, the sweet words of a Gru Grimm preacher drew her in. He promised he could numb her pain, erase her history, and give her the power to do as she pleased, as long as she would swear her life to his mysterious god. She did -- and soon came to find that in doing so, she swore away her very free will.

Nowadays, she'd sooner swallow coals than follow orders. Sure, not everyone in authority is as evil as the cult that stole her youth, but she isn't taking any more chances. She is more than happy to help her friends -- but she will never spill blood again for what she doesn't believe is right.

entries.name: Elmina
entries.title: Void Renegade
entries.description:
"Elmina, Elmina! I patched the hole in your shirt for you!"

"Aww, kiddo. You didn't have to, I was going to take care of that tonight myself."

"I know. But you've been so busy with your new friends. I thought you'd want to spend some more time with them instead of doing chores at home."

"Honestly, doing chores at home sounds like a dream right now. How do you think my shirt got a hole in the first place?"

"Were you playing too hard with your friends?"

"Sure. We were playing. With pointy sticks and monsters. Speaking of which, never go out at night without me, okay?"

entries.name: Yuki
entries.title: Born of Winter
entries.description:
Born at the height of a historic snow storm, Yuki has always had coldness flowing through her veins. As a baby, she would summon snow flurries to rock her own cradle. As a child, she would freeze a nearby lake to go ice skating at the height of summer. Her friends and family marveled at her powers...for a time. But the human heart is a fearful and jealous one, unable to abide what it can't understand. On the eve of her eighteenth birthday, the village elders exiled her, swearing to her a swift sword to the neck should she return.

Their mistake was in thinking that she'd ever want to.

entries.name: Yuki
entries.title: Born of Winter
entries.description:
Yuki made work for herself as a wandering mage for several years. She flitted from noble to noble who wanted the services of the famed Winter Witch, either for entertainment or for war. The spectacle of animating ice sculptures and burying opposing armies under snow was amusing, but without fail, she would grow bored with her master's mundane tasks and set off to find another. Erden, it seemed, held little of interest to a living force of nature. That is, until the Pandemonium began.

Rody and his friends approached her with neither fear nor gold, only a request that she use her immense power to help slay the Eternal. The prospect intrigued Yuki enough for her to comply.

If she'd known how their quest would end, she'd never have dared.

entries.name: Fornaxos
entries.title: Flame Incarnate
entries.description:
Long ago, in the time of the first Pandemonium, Fornaxos inherited the power of flame and became one of the six Legendary Dragons that embody the elements of Erden. Tasked with defeating the Eternal, he knew that untempered fire would destroy more than it would save. In his humility, he retreated to a lone mountain to hone his power and train for the battle to come.

Settlers who ventured too far up that mountain would return bearing tales of a shining creature who split the night sky with pillars of flame. Thus, the villagers came to call their home Mount Fornax, in honor of the dragon who shared it with them. Most have now forgotten why the rocky peak bears that name...but just enough of the story survives to inspire travelers to make the climb, in the hope that they'll see a legend in the flesh.

entries.name: Fornaxos
entries.title: Flame Incarnate
entries.description:
Fornaxos spent centuries alone, preparing himself to one day face the godlike Eternal. Now that the threat is vanquished, he finds himself a bit perplexed at his existence. If his purpose was to end the Pandemonium, and a group of mortals completed that purpose for him, what right does he have to call himself a legendary guardian of Erden?

After some thought, he has decided to leave the lonely summit of Mount Fornax to explore the world beyond. His brethren, the five other Legendary Dragons, must be facing the same conundrum that he is, now that the world is free. Perhaps with them by his side, he can forge a new legend to fulfill.

entries.name: Antares
entries.title: Flametongue Maid
entries.description:
During the third Pandemonium, the legendary Fire Dragon Fornaxos saved a human man from certain death. To show his eternal gratitude, the man swore that his bloodline would stand guard over and sanctify Fornaxos's mountain until the end of days. Thus, Antares' ancestor decided her fate centuries before she was born.

She doesn't begrudge him this, of course. Antares has always been happy to have a purpose, even if it was one she never chose herself. Still, some days she finds her eyes wandering to the village at the foot of the mountain, far from the sacred ground she cares for all alone.

Just who are these people that Fornaxos works so hard to protect?

entries.name: Antares
entries.title: Flametongue Maid
entries.description:
Now that Fornaxos has freed her of her ancestor's vow, Antares finds herself fascinated by the many peoples and cultures of Erden. Like a firefly on the breeze, she flits from place to place, learning all she can.

As a former recluse, however, she never had to learn to tame the considerable fire magic within her. This has caused its fair share of difficulties in her travels. For instance, one bikki village forbade her to return after she dried up all its mud baths in a single day.

If you know any tutors of fire magic that don't mind getting a few burns, please let her know.

entries.name: Crescent
entries.title: Fledgling Shadow
entries.description:
"Bad boy, Crescent! Bad! Bad!"

"Kyuu..."

"What'd the dragon do this time?"

"I hope you didn't want dinner tonight, Solas, because our eggs have vanished."

"What do you mean? Did they roll out of your basket again?"

"Of course not! I was just putting the pepper on my omelette when Crescent sneezed on it, or something. This black stuff came out of his mouth instead of fire --"

"Black stuff? Like tar?"

"No, like a shadow. Or a bit of night. I don't know. Anyway, it hit the eggs and made them disappear, so Crescent's not getting any more grapes for a week. Hmph."

"Kyuu..."

entries.name: Crescent
entries.title: Fledgling Shadow
entries.description:
"Hey, Luna. Can I talk to you about Crescent?"

"Okay...but why? I thought I finally got him housebroken."

"You really haven't, but that's not why I'm worried. You know that shadow stuff he can breathe when he's surprised or angry?"

"Yeah?"

"I just found a book about dragons at the library. I tried to look up Crescent's species, but there wasn't a single mention of dragons who could breathe shadow. Not anywhere."

"Really? That's unbelievable..."

"I know. Given where we found him, we have to consider that Crescent might not be a normal --"

"Why would a library keep such an incomplete tome of monsters on its shelves?! Such unprofessionalism! I'm going to lodge a complaint."

"Luna..."

entries.name: Ludmilla
entries.title: Crimson Valkyrie
entries.description:
Among the noble houses of Rigland, House Aldyne boasts one of the longest and proudest pedigrees, and some of the fiercest warriors ever to face the Pandemonium.
Ludmilla, the family's only daughter, was raised to be demure, retiring, and modest; but her family's blood burned strong in her.
A noble soul from birth, she stood up against injustice, even to adults, and protected the weak when they could not protect themselves, despite her parents' protests.
Somehow, it still came as a surprise to her family when she ran away from her sheltered life, joined the Red Dragon Knights, and stepped into the history books as a champion.

entries.name: Ludmilla
entries.title: Crimson Valkyrie
entries.description:
"Hey, Boss! We got trouble! The Red Dragon Knights were spotted in a village nearby!"
"Calm down. We just need to make ourselves scarce before—"
"Too late! Throw down your weapons, bandit scum!"
"What?! The Crimson Valkyrie?!"
"But how?! That village is hours from here!"
"Unfortunately for you, we run this far before breakfast every morning. In armor. Isn't that right?"
"R-right, Captain..."
"Damn...we'll have to fight! They'll be tired from the march! Kill 'em!"
"How considerate! We won't have to chase you down. Red Dragon Knights, draw arms!"

entries.title: Blade of the Aeons
entries.description:
"So! You're that "demon" Norza that's been causing trouble around here!"
"...And you are?"
"They call me Matias... Matias the Flamebearer! I hear you've been seeking out skilled warriors, boy. Well, your predations end here and now! Draw your sword. I challenge you to—gaaaah!"
"Spare me the speech. If you mean to kill, do it quickly and quietly...mm. I guess you can't hear me anymore."

entries.name: Norza
entries.title: Blade of the Aeons
entries.description:
The young man traveled the land, seeking the strongest warriors he could find.
As he was already a peerless swordsman, most duels ended quickly. On the rare occasions he was at a disadvantage, he would retreat and return again the next day, until he won.

Those he defeated, he slew without mercy. True power can only be found beyond the boundary of life and death.
Standing over the bodies of his opponents filled him with a rush of power.

Still not enough. I can get stronger still. I must get stronger still. I must defeat stronger foes.
The avarice in his heart drew him ever onward, fleeing from the powerlessness he felt that day.

entries.name: Druke
entries.title: Apex Blademaster
entries.description:
Druke's father was a skilled blacksmith, his name known across South Rigland.
Warriors would come from all over, crossing the brutal Habsu Desert just to buy his wares.
However, his son possessed no skill for the forge at all.
Though he worried for his son, the blacksmith took a local boy, Gilgan, as his apprentice and successor, and Druke lived his teenage years shiftless and ill-content.
However...one night, a bandit snuck into the forge while the boys' parents were out.
Gilgan advised caution, but Druke seized one of his father's blades from the wall and struck the thief down.
It was as if Druke had cut the haze from his thoughts with the same blow.
Though his parents scolded him, Druke felt certain. Forging weapons was not the only way to live by the sword.

entries.name: Druke
entries.title: Apex Blademaster
entries.description:
Rody: "We'll need to do something about that dragon if we want to get through here. What's the plan?"
Callen: "I have an idea. I can concoct some sleeping medicine. If we put it in some meat and leave it out as bait..."
Druke: "That'll take way too long. I say we run in there and kill the stupid thing."
Callen: "Don't be ridiculous! That thing will tear you to—"
Rody: "No, he's got a point... we are in a hurry, after all. Let's not overthink this."
Callen: "Not you too!"
Rody: "Look, it's not even a dragon god, it's just a regular dragon. If we can't kill it, how can we hope to fight the Eternal?"
Callen: "...Well...I mean... Yuki, say something to these idiots!"
Yuki: "Just a flying lizard. It'll be no match for my ice."
Callen: "You... you meatheads!"

entries.name: Greta
entries.title: Storm-Inspired Inventor
entries.description:
Greta is going to change the world someday. Everyone in her village told her so when she was a child, so it must be true. She has everything a visionary needs: a whip-smart mind, a tireless work ethic, and perhaps most importantly, a total lack of common sense. She dreams of one day capturing lightning in a bottle, so that the people of Erden can have as much light as they want, whenever they want.

A traveling Winvy merchant -- who has gone by many names, but who she knows as Mr. Beauregard Chancy -- saw the potential of her idea when she chased him down at the market fair one day. He agreed to put her through sorcery school in exchange for the exclusive right to sell her invention once she has a working model.

It's been three years. It must be coming any day now.

entries.name: Greta
entries.title: Storm-Inspired Inventor
entries.description:
There's nothing Greta loves more than a good thunderstorm. She goes out in them to take notes whenever she can; she'll drag herself out in her pajamas, if she has to. You must first understand something to tame it, and after three years, Greta understands lightning as much as anyone can. Still, the pouring rain and rolling thunder relax her, so she continues studying anyway.

Storms have always been her friend. Once, when she was very young, she fell asleep reading by candlelight. The wax dripped onto her straw bed, and in minutes, her home went up in flames. Only a passing thundercloud saved her family's lives. She'll never forget how bright the lightning was that split the sky that night.

She's chased it ever since.

entries.name: Endless Paladin
entries.title: Champion of the Black Flames
entries.description:
The servants of Orcanix know no death. Should they be reduced to dust, their master will summon them to her Pyre and create their bodies anew. Even so, nothing can be destroyed again and again without consequence.

The Paladins have no flesh, no blood. Some have lost even their bones, existing more as ghostly wraiths than moving corpses. They would wear their twisted husks with pride, if they could feel such things. But rebirth demands a price far beyond the physical, and they have paid in full.

Nothing remains inside them but bloodlust and blind obedience.

entries.name: Endless Paladin
entries.title: Champion of the Black Flames
entries.description:
The armies of Orcanix revere their Paladins almost as much as they revere the goddess herself. Some have existed for centuries, before the war with the mortal kingdoms. Some have shed the blood of kings, some have slaughtered dragons. All leave legends in their wake.

No coward can become a Paladin. They are tempered by death as steel is tempered by flame. To be a Paladin is to embrace suffering, to welcome and inflict it in equal measure. The goddess lets her monsters lead Erden's conquest, and they have never left her wanting. But she keeps her most elite for a secret purpose, far beyond what her underlings can fathom.

entries.name: Altaireon
entries.title: Soul of the Storm
entries.description:
When the Primordial Dragon of Storm fell in the first battle with the Eternal, he died too quickly to choose his successor. Desperately, he let a portion of his power fly into the world, to choose and grow within whatever it might find. It was reckless. It could spell disaster if the power of the heavens landed in the wrong hands. But he had no other choice.

As it happened, a hatchling crawled out of his cave to hunt for scraps. He had been cast away from his brethren, too small and skittish to call himself a dragon. Had he hid a moment longer, he might have died a runt, forgotten and inconsequential. But that was not to be his fate.

Altaireon stepped beneath the open sky, and the power of the storm struck him like a bolt of lightning.

entries.name: Altaireon
entries.title: Soul of the Storm
entries.description:
For many years, he resented and feared his new powers. He could barely control the thunder in his veins, and worse, he had done nothing to earn it. But after a century of fleeing the storms that dogged his steps, Altaireon decided he could run no more. Erden cried out for a savior to break the brutal cycle of the Pandemonium. He was not worthy to be that savior -- not yet. But he could become worthy. He owed it to the heavens that had gifted him their fury.

The young Legendary Dragon sharpened his claws and got to work.

Success did not come quickly. A storm resists taming by its very nature; if he isn't worthy, even a dragon will find himself at the mercy of the winds. After months of fruitless effort, Altaireon found a group of mortal monks living secluded in the mountains. Though they had no power of their own, and no assurance of immortality, they sought whatever perfection they could achieve in their lives. Some wrote poetry, others performed martial arts. Some only meditated from dusk until dawn, pondering the reason for existence. These were not the cowardly mortals Altaireon had seen rise since the fall of the dragons. These were a cut above the rest. In some ways, they were even more than him.

He humbly submitted himself to their teaching, and though he lived to see the youngest of them wither and die, he could not have mastered the tumult of the storm without them.

entries.name: Endless Rizette
entries.title: Angel of Ruin
entries.description:
She doesn't know how many times she has died. They all blur together, a haze of pain, darkness, and brutal rebirth. There's no point in remembering them all, not when she'll live until the end of time.

But there was one death that mattered. Her first death. The one that should have been her last. She would give anything to forget, but it burns like a torch in her mind, as eternal as her master's black fire.

If Finn shares her tormented memory, he has never shown it. Beneath his twisted shell, he might have no memory at all.

entries.name: Endless Rizette
entries.title: Angel of Ruin
entries.description:
None of them knew it would go so wrong.

The odds were always stacked against them. But Rei had gifted them her magic. Callen had forged their hero a holy sword. The people of Erden cheered for their victory, sending them off to the Endless Pyre with celebrations and songs.

For a time, they thought they had a chance.

They were fools to ever think so.

Gods are gods and men are men. Even with a legendary blade, Finn was doomed to fall. Looking back, Rizette wonders if she should have stood her ground. She wonders what might have happened if she hadn't given her life for his.

Would he still have betrayed them all?

entries.name: Elmina
entries.title: Yuletide Rogue
entries.description:
"Hey! Watch where you're going, you nearly made me drop my stuff!"

"What are you rushing around for, anyway? All the good stuff in this market's already been snatched up. Don't tell me you're one of those people who put their shopping off to the last minute."

"...sigh. Of course you are. I should have guessed."

"Well, don't just stand there. Follow me. I know a couple places that can help you out, but you better not do this again, you hear? The people in your life must really care about you. What's the point of Christmas if you don't give them something special?"
