entries.name: Elmina
entries.title: Yuletide Rogue
entries.description:
"Merry Christ -- what? You again? How'd you find out where I live?! If you followed me home, I swear..."

"One of the kids let you know, huh? I keep telling them not to bother with strangers, but they never seem to listen."

"Hey, you weren't a stranger, you were -- a charity case! You were wandering around so lost, it would have been bad karma to leave you on your own! Anyway, why did you even come here?"

"...to give me a thank you present. Yeah, right. There isn't a thing in the city left to buy."

"Huh? You made it yourself?"

"..."

"Guess it'd be even more bad karma to turn you away now. Fine, fine, come in. But watch where you're going. I didn't buy all these gifts just to have 'em stepped on."

entries.name: Rolotia
entries.title: Timeless Beauty
entries.description:
As the avatar of Chronos, Rolotia dutifully watches over the year as it passes away. She feels the weariness of the land, strewn with bare forests and blanketed under snow. She sleeps by the side of hibernating animals, holed up in their dens. She knows, as the world does, that the end will soon arrive.

Quiet as a whisper, the world will begin anew. She will see this year from its winter birth to the heights of its summer, and though it too will die someday, she greets its first day with a smile.

entries.name: Rolotia
entries.title: Timeless Beauty
entries.description:
Many people start the new year with self-reflection. With resolute hearts, they set out to be new people, free of the vices that have held them back. Rolotia observes these resolutions with glee, even knowing most of them will be broken. The first days of a year have a purity to them, a power that makes any dream appear within reach. If a single person sees their promises through, none of their failed resolutions were wasted.

She's no stranger to wanting a second chance.

entries.name: Shanna
entries.title: Snowkissed Maid
entries.description:
When she set out on her mercenary journey, Shanna thought she would only learn the ways of combat. As it happens, she's discovered Christmas too.

The human holiday mystifies her a bit. The saru are a generous people, but they are also practical. They exchange gifts all year long with little fanfare. So why do humans tie them up with bows and leave them under a tree? Why do they build cookie houses without eating them? Why do people gather to sing songs in the snow? Aren't they cold?

There's much she still needs to learn, but she thinks she likes this strange celebration of theirs.

entries.name: Shanna
entries.title: Snowkissed Maid
entries.description:
"Oh, you made it! Welcome!"

"Do you want some eggnog? A candy cane? How about a gingerbread man?"

"The market didn't have any more turkies, so I caught one myself! Did I make it right? It's over there, next to the pancakes. Go sit by the fire, I'll bring you some!"

"Huh? Why did I make all of this for you? It's Christmas, isn't it?"

"...n-not for two more days?"

"..."

"Just think of it like a Christmas ambush, okay?"

entries.name: Rizette
entries.title: Spring's First Blossom
entries.description:
Rizette has never spent a New Year in the capital.

She's heard the stories, of course. Dancers puppeting magnificent dragons weave up and down the streets; revelers light the moonless sky with fireworks and lantern light. Families will travel from across the kingdom to spend one night at home, and everyone's made so much food, not a soul goes hungry.

To a country girl like her, it sounds too exciting to be real. But she does have a question.

What is she to wear on a night as grand as that?

entries.name: Rizette
entries.title: Spring's First Blossom
entries.description:
Rizette's hometown welcomed the New Year warmly, but with little fanfare. She remembers the fragrant bread and soup her mother would make, how she and Theo would run around the house, banishing evil spirits with brooms and feather dusters. After dinner, with bellies full and fire crackling, her father would lean back in his chair and read from their book of fairy tales. Theo always fell asleep before he finished.

Compared to the lanterns and fireworks of the capital, the New Year of her home is nothing. But she misses it all the same.

entries.name: Voraxion
entries.title: Adamantine Maw
entries.description:
Long, long ago, a destitute kingdom fell under siege. Fearing his people's poverty would mean their downfall, the king commissioned a group of alchemists to craft him an endless source of gold.

After some months, the alchemists presented him with a tiny beast. In front of him, they fed it scrap metal, spoiled food, all manner of the kingdom's waste -- and he watched as horns of solid gold grew on its body.

The king wept for joy, for with this monster, he'd saved his kingdom.

Or so he thought.

entries.name: Voraxion
entries.title: Adamantine Maw
entries.description:
Months passed. The relentless war continued, and even with the gold from the beast's severed horns, the kingdom could not push the enemy back. Desperate, the king begged the alchemists to push the creature further. To feed it more and more. To curse it with hunger so great, it could never stop eating.

The alchemists warned the king that such a curse could have consequences more dire than any war, but he wouldn't listen. They'd given this monster life themselves. Surely, if it ever turned on them, they could force it back into servitude.

entries.name: Shiori
entries.title: Steel-Edged Sakura
entries.description:
There was a world before the wall.

A world before black rain, and dragonfire, and rations that never last. A world before the undead, where the sky shone bright and the spring brought flowers.

She has never seen such things herself. The monstrous goddess started her war before she was ever born, leaving her with nothing but legends and tales of days gone by. Most these days don't have even that, so beaten down by the undead siege that every hope has fled them.

But not her. Never her.

Even knowing they will die, the sakuras bloom. And so will she.

entries.name: Shiori
entries.title: Steel-Edged Sakura
entries.description:
The war with the undead started eighteen years ago, but the history of her clan stretches much, much further. Though her village was little more than a farming settlement by the time it fell, it guarded an ancient samurai fortress. The last lord to reside in that fortress met his death at the hands of the undead. Now, like everything else, it sits abandoned.

Shiori has never set foot inside it herself. As bodyguards of the samurai, her family was not permitted within its walls without their master's blessing.

But sometimes in her dreams, she travels to its gates to stand watch.

entries.name: Alira
entries.title: Silk-Veiled Siren
entries.description:
―― Beginning mission report.

I've just departed Hymelian territory. Duke Valenne welcomed me to his feast most warmly, and his son even more so. I fortuitously happened by his bedroom in the morning -- just in time to be caught. Your wisdom proved invaluable yet again, master. The flustered lover truly is my most convincing mask.

The rumor mill has already started churning. As you requested, the boy is unharmed, though I can't say the same for his reputation. I expect his betrothed's house to call things off within the month, which is a problem solved for us.

I remain your faithful servant. For the glory of Bergaria.

entries.name: Alira
entries.title: Silk-Veiled Siren
entries.description:
―― Beginning mission report.

I thank you for this assignment, master. Every day I am away from Bergaria, I long to return. To work the Emperor's will in his own domain is my highest honor.

The upstart senator was bold to throw a ball while so publicly defying his regent. I've seen to his punishment. The toxin I slipped into his wine shrivels the body from the inside out. Make no mistake: everyone will know he was poisoned, and everyone will know why.

I remain your faithful servant. For the glory of Bergaria.

entries.name: Endless Phantom
entries.title: Shadeborn Menace
entries.description:
In a war on life itself, every death is a victory. Whether claimed in glory or claimed in the dark, it matters not; only that another soul has been brought into the fold. Few have shepherded more souls to the Goddess than the dreaded Endless Phantoms.

Some say the shadowy assassins armor themselves in the bones of their victims. Whether that's true, none can say. They move through the night indistinguishable as shadow, and all who come close enough to see the make of their armor are already good as dead.

entries.name: Endless Phantom
entries.title: Shadeborn Menace
entries.description:
Every so often, a new undead convert catches the goddess's eye. An honored knight, a well-respected mage, a particularly clever thief; when these souls succumb to her black flame, she molds their bodies for a special purpose.

The Endless Phantoms bear no trace of their previous lives. They possess no will of their own and rarely speak. No one living knows why they were chosen for their grim task. Some believe they emerged, whole and monstrous, from the Endless Pyre itself. But they are wrong.

Orcanix's assassins were as monstrous in life as they are in undeath.

entries.name: Aethrodon
entries.title: Gossamer Drake
entries.description:
"Day 26, on the road to elven lands:

I have slept in my saddle now for three days straight. Our caravan leader insists he will not stop until he spies a baby Aethrodon -- the blasted things appear only in total safety, he says. Camping anywhere else would doom us all, he says.

Confound these elves. Pandemonium or not, we're armed to the teeth. I think we can take our chances, tiny lizard's blessing be damned."

-- From The Travels of Caerwyn

entries.name: Aethrodon
entries.title: Gossamer Drake
entries.description:
"Day 31, in the heart of the elven forests:

I'd seen nothing like it in all my days. Bedecked in bronze armor, brilliant as a shard of the summer sky -- this, I asked, is what comes of those skittish creatures we glimpsed on the road?

'Why else would we wait to see one before we camp?' the chieftain answered me. 'Wherever a young Aethrodon wanders, its mother guards its path. No enemy would dare approach.'"

-- From The Travels of Caerwyn

entries.name: Endless Ludmilla
entries.title: Savior of the Living City
entries.description:
No one has arrived late to an inspection in six months. No one would dare. Not with the Queen of Valkyries heading the Vanguard.

As a city under constant siege, Last Bastion can't afford imperfection in its soldiers, as she so frequently reminds them. A single mistake could cost lives. Either her men rise to her standards, or they forfeit their place in her service. Time is a precious resource, and Ludmilla will waste none of it on mercy.

The goddess certainly won't.

This will not activate if the unit begins its turn burning. Any unit that deals damage to or is dealt damage by a unit with burn will also become burned.

entries.name: Endless Ludmilla
entries.title: Savior of the Living City
entries.description:
She knows they call her a tyrant. In the shadows of the city she fights so hard to protect, she knows her people curse her name.

They have their reasons: hunger, cold, exhaustion. She was but a child when the invasion began, but the living complained of the very same in the world before. Perhaps some things never change. Or perhaps, no matter their circumstances, people find a way to be discontent.

It doesn't matter. Let them mutter and curse as much as they please. As long as they survive their wretched nights, she has done her duty.

entries.name: Endless Astrid
entries.title: Voice of Defiance
entries.description:
There is no rebellion in Last Bastion. There is no rebellion in Last Bastion. There is no rebellion in Last Bastion.

The Vanguard like to think it will become true if they say it enough, but they know they are kidding themselves. A year ago, the missing supplies, the vandalism, even the accidents could be waved away as the work of disorganized malcontents. Today, they are too frequent, too directed against the city's tyrannical leader. A reckoning, long overdue, will soon arrive.

She will not be silenced now. She has waited far too long.

entries.name: Endless Astrid
entries.title: Voice of Defiance
entries.description:
Stories of the rebel leader pervade Last Bastion's populace, whispered so as not to attract a passing patrol's attention. Spreading rumors of subversion is tantamount to treason in a city that depends so heavily on its unity, but even so, the lure of the forbidden keeps Astrid's infamy alive.

The stories say she can take on six soldiers at once, that she can move without a sound, that no one has ever seen her sleep. But for every believer who marvels at her deeds, another skeptic scoffs.

Surely if she possessed such power, she would have already won.

entries.name: Cyrus
entries.title: Mercurial Mastermind
entries.description:
The Devil. Fifteenth Arcana of the Tarot. The card of duplicity, corruption, and inescapable fate.

Cyrus learned to read the cards as a boy, the poor bastard of a lord. Even as a child, he was no fool; his use to his father would end the moment his wife bore him a true heir. Rather than wait to be unceremoniously murdered, he ran away and joined an underground ring of thieves masquerading as soothsayers and fortune tellers. His skill with a deck served him well, but unlike his comrades, Cyrus told no lies.

The misfortunes he predicted had a way of coming true.

entries.name: Cyrus
entries.title: Mercurial Mastermind
entries.description:
The Tower. Sixteenth Arcana of the Tarot. The card of suffering, disaster, and unforeseen consequences.

It was a simple enough job. A mercenary named Rody hired him to investigate a collapsing mine. Nothing more than a tidy purse of gold should have come of it. But somehow, Cyrus found himself embroiled in a fight to save the world.

The others at Rody's side were good, honest. Not like him. They played their parts in the grand pantomime well, but he could see the Pandemonium for what it was. A scheme.

Somewhere, someone was pulling their strings, and when everything finally went wrong, he vowed to find out who.

entries.name: Rizette
entries.title: Lightbearing Leader
entries.description:
They came in numbers Rigland couldn't hope to match. They armed themselves with technology the world had never seen. And when the time came to stand against them, the Red Dragon Knights crumbled from the inside. Not even at the height of the Pandemonium had the future seemed so bleak.

But Rizette had no choice but to move forward.

With her brother at her side, she fled the fallen capital and returned to her hometown: the ruined Westcarte, where her adventure began. Once more, in a place of desolation, hope would rise anew.

This will overwrite other status effects on the user. For 150TU, the next time the unit with counter stance takes damage from an enemy attack, it will become their turn. Counter stance cannot be gained if the user already has the Counter Stance status effect or has gotten the current turn from Counter Stance. This skill becomes locked for 200TU after use.

entries.name: Rizette
entries.title: Lightbearing Leader
entries.description:
Though her kingdom is conquered, its people aren't yet crushed. No matter what indignities the Bergarian soldiers force upon them, no matter the oppression they face, they know that somewhere out there, someone is fighting for their freedom. And they want to aid her however they can.

At the very whisper of Rizette's presence, crates of food and weapons appear at her door. Recruits band together, crude weapons in hand, untrained but ready to fight.

Her rebellion may be young, as fragile as a fawn finding its legs, but as long as Rigland's spirit burns bright, she knows she has nothing to fear.

entries.name: Gloria
entries.title: Gilded Heiress
entries.description:
Gloria Valen, daughter of Maximilius Valen, was born under a lucky star. Her mother told her so every day of her childhood. Gloria believed it until the day she died, leaving her in the sole care of her cruel and miserly father. Somehow, her stars never seemed so lucky after that.

She never wanted for material comfort. Her father's profound wealth bought her the finest cuisine, the most elegant fashion, and invitations to every noble estate in Bergaria. For many it would have been enough, but like any true heiress, she dreamed of more.

She dreamed of a life worth more than her family name.

entries.name: Gloria
entries.title: Gilded Heiress
entries.description:
Unlike most Bergarian nobility, Gloria's father gathered his fortune not through inheritance, but through business. His eye for rich alchemical ore made him a talented magical merchant, selling enchanted weapons to the highest bidder regardless of allegiance or creed. Unsurprisingly, this earned him many enemies; Gloria grew up dodging kidnappers and assassins alike.

Lord Valen always believed his daughter's greatest worth to him would be her eventual marriage. He never saw the way she tinkered with his cast-off gems. He never saw the way she found beauty and power in what he rejected.

But one day -- maybe thanks to that lucky star of hers -- someone else did.

entries.name: Lillian
entries.title: Spritely Spearmaster
entries.description:
Lillian Rosalinde von Dreyer was born with a certain problem. Since its founding, her bloodline has been known for two things: indefatigable spirit and truly enormous size. The story goes that, after giving birth to five strapping boys, her mother swore that her sixth would be the strongest warrior of them all. So, naturally, Lillian arrived not only small for her family, but small for any Hymelian.

Only the foolish dare bring up this little legend around her, however. She tends to challenge anyone who does to a duel, to prove that her mother's prediction was in no way false.

entries.name: Lillian
entries.title: Spritely Spearmaster
entries.description:
Lillian's father worried that having five elder brothers might overwhelm a dainty little girl. However, his worry soon proved to be quite unfounded. More often than not, Lillian was the one who sent her brothers running, beating them back from her dolls with a tree branch spear. When they heard of the Hymelian princess's penchant for combat, Lillian's parents sent her to be her handmaiden -- or perhaps, more accurately, her apprentice.

 Though Maxima fights with a sword instead of a spear, Lillian has taken her aggressive philosophy quite to heart. In battle, she ensures her opponents never gain an inch on her allies, no matter what she has to do.

entries.name: Diabolos
entries.title: Dragon Lord of Shadow
entries.description: His name is Crescent, and he's a very happy dragon. Or at least, he used to be. He doesn't remember all that much. One day, there was nothing but the dark. The next day, there were Luna, and Solas, and the girl with the fluffy white hair. And grapes! He had no reason not to be happy in a world with grapes and good friends. But one day, that stopped being enough. Something inside him, like a shadow on a moonless night, started following him. Whispering to him. There's more than this. More than this. Remember. Crescent doesn't know if he wants to. He thinks, if he does, he might never be happy again.

entries.name: Diabolos
entries.title: Dragon Lord of Shadow
entries.description: Diabolos... The voice in Crescent's mind haunts him with that name. Diabolos. Not Crescent. Diabolos. You are Diabolos. He doesn't want that awful name. It sounds ancient, larger than he is, cursed with history he can't remember. Suddenly, his days with Luna and Solas seem so small, a speck of happiness against a shadow that threatens to consume him. He wants to protect his friends. He wants to stand by their side forever. But if he is Diabolos, and if the shadow inside him one day shows him its secrets... Will they even want to be near him?

entries.name: Kisara
entries.title: Blade of the Summer Sun
entries.description:
The villagers called little Kisara the terror of their town.

No corner of their farming hamlet was spared her whirlwind mischief. The young girl practically vibrated with energy -- energy her traditional, rule-abiding parents couldn't handle. The more they forced her into chores and quiet reading, the more she rebelled, mouthing off to anyone who'd listen and breaking valuable tools in the name of playing 'samurai.' 

At their wits' end, her mother and father attempted to betroth her to a wealthy merchant in the city, but a wandering swordsman instead asked for the chance to take Kisara on as his apprentice. He alone saw the burning heart her misbehavior masked.

entries.name: Kisara
entries.title: Blade of the Summer Sun
entries.description:
At first, Kisara rebelled against her master's strictness. She had an undeniable talent for the sword. He'd even told her so. Why did he insist on repetitive drills, boring meals, and hours upon hours of meditation?

He replied that meditation nourished her mind, rice nourished her body, and drills nourished her blade. Should any one of these things go dull, all the talent in the world couldn't keep her alive.

Eventually, she saw the wisdom of his words, and channeled her boundless energy into a signature fighting style. Long before she left her master, tales of her unrelenting speed and strength made her enemies tremble.

entries.name: Rolotia
entries.title: Sorceress of Dawn
entries.description:
In ancient times, Bergaria was not a vast, imposing empire, but a scattered collection of tribes. The harsh mountains left little room to farm, so famines came both fierce and frequent. If they hoped to survive, peasants would have to swear their service to a warlord, turning their eyes away from the cruelty they inflicted.

The priestesses of Chronos, hidden in a northern temple, were spared their land's turmoil. In exchange for keeping watch over time's flow, the Primordial granted them both safety and power. In all of Erden, only they could manipulate time. 

Rolotia, gifted with the most prodigious time magic seen in generations, always felt that fate was more a curse than a blessing.

entries.name: Rolotia
entries.title: Sorceress of Dawn
entries.description:
As a child, Rolotia drove the other priestesses to no end of frustration. Though she was never malicious, never drunk on her own power, she proved too spirited to abide by the order's rules of isolation. Her eyes forever strayed to the horizon. Had she not been such a prodigy, perhaps the elders might have let her go. But doing so would have been too great a risk.

Before long, their indecision became their undoing. One morning, when the priestesses went to wake Rolotia for prayer, they found her staff gone and her bedroom empty. She had frozen time and escaped into the world so long forbidden to her.

entries.name: Gemina
entries.title: Devoted Timekeeper
entries.description:
Deep within the northernmost mountains of Bergaria, an order of secretive priestesses makes their home. Few know of their existence, and even fewer could locate them if pressed. Long ago, Chronos herself gifted their ancestors with power over time, a gift she bestowed only on them. If time magic were to spread among the peoples of Erden, history itself could dissolve into a meaningless paradox.

And so it is the priestesses' duty to watch the flow of time, ever neutral, ever detached from the world. It is a peaceful life, free of worry. Few acolytes find reason to complain.

But Gemina's curiosity was much too great to let her remain an observer.

entries.name: Gemina
entries.title: Devoted Timekeeper
entries.description:
Gemina heard legends as she was growing up. Cautionary tales of a priestesses who ran from the temple and caused untold disaster. Her elders used their tragic fates to scare the acolytes into compliance. Gemina, however, would not be swayed.

She understood the importance of her order's neutrality. Those trusted to oversee time itself had a great responsibility, greater than most in Erden would ever have to face. But how could the guardians of history perform their duty if the world was nothing to them but a collection of sad stories?

That is why she ran away. Gemina escaped the priestesses' temple not to defile her duty, but to embrace its truest purpose.

entries.name: Bahamut
entries.title: Doomsday Dragon God
entries.description: The Dragon God's name is written in ancient legends. The leader of all dragons, the Bringer of the End - Bahamut. For a time, he was thought to be mere legend— the possibility that he might truly exist, a nightmare too horrible to contemplate. A single dragon can destroy a town; what would their god be capable of?

—An ancient inscription

entries.name: Jeanne d'Arc
entries.title: Maiden of Radiance
entries.description:
Jeanne and Mikaela d'Arc were born a strange set of twins. Mikaela arrived first, a few minutes before the last midnight of winter, while Jeanne came in the first hours of spring. The town priestess insisted the birth was an omen from His Radiance, though she never said what it foretold.

Though technically the same age, it seemed only natural to call Mikaela the older sister. She was, after all, the more outgoing one, the braver one. The town cooed over her, the little warrior, so devoted to seeing Jeanne safe and happy.

And Jeanne loved Mikaela more than anyone in the world.

entries.name: Jeanne d'Arc
entries.title: Maiden of Radiance
entries.description:
Though Jeanne often stayed in the shadow of her sister, the village noticed her piety. She obeyed the church's teachings, prayed often, and helped who she could in her own shy way. Many suspected she would become the priestess's acolyte when she came of age, just as they said Mikaela would join the holy army.

Sadly, neither would come to pass.

When the war took a turn for the worse, their village found itself alone in the dark, without defense. Jeanne and Mikaela ran for the church together, hoping that if they reached its sacred ground, they'd be safe.

They were right: the demons couldn't breach the church. But only Jeanne made it there alive.

entries.name: Eveline
entries.title: Pious Acolyte
entries.description:
It is a terrible thing to grow up in war. But while Eveline was young, the demons seemed so far away. The type of thing only soldiers worried about. The power of His Radiance and the Holy Maiden kept the rest of them safe.

Mikaela, Eveline's longtime friend, always boasted that she would join the army when she grew up. Eveline wished she could share her courage. She longed to serve her god and her kingdom, but her strength was never in swordsmanship. It was in her piety, which was rivaled only by little Jeanne, and in her wit, which was rivaled by none.

entries.name: Eveline
entries.title: Pious Acolyte
entries.description:
When the demons finally swept through their village, she tried to help Jeanne through her loss. Mikaela had given strength to them both, but as important as she'd been to Eveline, to Jeanne, she was irreplaceable. And now she was gone.

In the end, the grief turned too much to bear. Eveline pledged herself as an acolyte to the church and gradually lost touch with her old friend. But she never forgot about her, and she never lost faith that someday, light would return to her life.

That faith would be rewarded a thousand times over in the years to come.

entries.name: Rolotia
entries.title: Lady of Eternal Summer
entries.description:
Growing up in the mountains of Bergaria, Rolotia knew only chilly springs, frost-kissed autumns, and winters so bitter they killed all memory of warmth.

Now that she's made it to the beach for the first time, she's as thrilled as she is overwhelmed. The sun shines so strongly she can feel its rays brush her skin. The sea stretches from her feet to the horizon, free and untamed, nothing like the icy peaks that shadowed her childhood.

If this is summer, she never wants it to end.

entries.name: Rolotia
entries.title: Lady of Eternal Summer
entries.description:
Rolotia loves the beach. She'd much rather race across golden sand than harsh, glinting snow. Unfortunately, sunshine and her pale northern skin don't get along well.

After that first day, which saw her going to bed a painful shade of crimson, Rolotia has come prepared. She persuaded Rizette to part with some sun-cloaking cream in exchange for a few magically extended moments with Finn. She made herself an oasis of shade with her umbrella. But what is she to do when she wants to join her friends in the water?

Perhaps a hat can keep the sun away.

entries.name: Elmina
entries.title: Sunkissed Mirage
entries.description:
People stare at Elmina no matter where she goes.

In human kingdoms, they stare because she's an elf. In the fair-skinned north, her tan draws curious attention. And when she's on the beach...well. She could do without the open-mouthed stares, but she likes bikinis best, so that's what she'll wear.

To tell the truth, being noticed takes some getting used to for a former street kid. That's why she learned to be stealthy. Keeping invisible saved her hide too many times to count, but it's no way to live. Not anymore.

She can slip into shadow whenever she wants. Why not enjoy a day in the sun?

entries.name: Elmina
entries.title: Sunkissed Mirage
entries.description:
Elmina didn't grow up with tropical heat, but she thinks it suits her. Humidity works wonders for her complexion, and the sun brings out the natural highlights in her hair. She doesn't have to worry about burns much either, though Callen still lectures her until she puts on proper protection.

Still, when the sun grows too bright even for her, she enjoys a cool retreat into the forest. Walking through the shade at the hottest time of day is a rare pleasure for one used to shivering in the cold.

And stumbling upon an unexpected waterfall? That's the kind of memory that begs to be shared with another.

entries.name: Alira
entries.title: Sweet Beach Temptation
entries.description: "My sweet angel, I'm heeeeere! We made it to the island at last. At last. I thought that horrible boat trip would never end. All those storms, all those people packed so close together. It's a miracle I didn't lose any of my things! (Don't worry, I checked my bags as soon as we docked. All my swimsuits are safe and sound∼) Wish you were with me, cuddlebug!" Shortly after landing at their island resort, a Hymelian noble was horrified to find her jewelry box had vanished. Alira stole it for the war effort, of course. But surely the emperor will let her keep a piece or two for herself.

entries.name: Alira
entries.title: Sweet Beach Temptation
entries.description:
"My most handsome darling,

Ugh. How gloomy everyone on this island is! Whenever I start to relax, someone has to bring up the invasion—or worse, the rebels. As soon as I get back, you owe me a kiss for each time I had to hear 'Blue Dragon Knights.' I'm warning you, it's a lot! ♡

Wish you were with me, pumpkin!"

In the middle of the night, resort guards arrested a wealthy merchant for treason. Supposedly, his secret correspondence with the Rigland resistance had been pinned to his door with knives. Alira usually prefers a more subtle approach, but this vacation put her in a more theatrical mood than usual.

entries.name: Antares
entries.title: Fiery Island Flower
entries.description: She's traveled from the great Mount Fornax to the sprawling ports of Zuthua. She's conquered the art of monster raising, learned fire magic, and even made a friend or two along the way. But now, Antares faces her most daunting challenge yet. Before the summer ends, she must learn to relax! Luna told her so the last time they met. Of course, she meant it as a joke, but never let it be said that Antares disappoints her idols. She stayed up all night researching the ideal vacation, and by the next morning, she was off to Laomis, a freshly sewn swimsuit in her bag.

entries.name: Antares
entries.title: Fiery Island Flower
entries.description:
Sitting by the pool with flowers in her hair and a picnic basket at her side, Antares may seem like a natural at this 'vacation' thing. But don't be fooled. Relaxing is harder than it looks.

Laomis has lived up to its reputation as a paradise. Everywhere she looks, there's another view to appreciate, another meal to try, another kind local to meet...she can barely keep up with it all! How will she ever experience the whole island before the season ends?

Oh. That's right: summer will come again soon. Maybe, bit by bit, year by year, she can become a vacation master after all.

entries.name: Callen
entries.title: Magical Dragon Girl
entries.description: Once, there was a girl known as a remarkable intellect even among the elven tribes. She was one of the very few chosen to work under the Celestial Lord in the laboratories of the Castle. Though arrogant, she had a strong sense of duty and righteousness, and swore to uphold her grand mission: to save the world.

entries.name: Callen
entries.title: Magical Dragon Girl
entries.description: However, it didn't take long for her to realize that the laboratory's purpose wasn't what she had in mind. She sought to rescue the world from the evil that plagued it; her superiors, however, only desired greater power and greater profit. The doubt in her heart grew day by day, until the day of catastrophe arrived, and her fears were realized.

entries.name: Olive
entries.title: Reclusive Witchling
entries.description:
The witches of Lumains know what it is to be feared, but that doesn't stop them from fearing. Even among outcasts, those witches who have taken on the features of a dragon are shunned. Olive understands why: witches only grow their horns when they've pushed their magic to the brink, losing themselves to the siren song of its power. A dragon witch has shown they're not to be trusted.

She's never told anyone how she got her horns and her lone wing. She's never told anyone how she lost her eye. It wouldn't matter anyway. The truth rarely changes anyone's mind.

entries.name: Olive
entries.title: Reclusive Witchling
entries.description:
Olive doesn't remember many things fondly, but she'll always treasure her mother's laugh. How she made the most delicious pastries. How she studied her spellbooks night and day, desperate to end the Holy Maiden's witch hunts.

It was that desperation that consumed her. Twisted into a dragon, more beast than woman, she attacked her own daughter, the very person she wanted most to save. In that struggle to protect herself, Olive grew her horns. If she hadn't, she doubts she would have survived.

Let the witches whisper that she's just like her mother, a disaster waiting to happen. She knows better than anyone the price of dark magic -- and knows too that sometimes, that price must be paid.

entries.name: Aria
entries.title: Petite Primordial
entries.description:
Aria, like all other Primordials, was born at the foundation of her world. Gifted with power over death, her duty was to judge mortal souls as they passed to the afterlife. With a touch, she could live each person's greatest triumphs and vilest sins, and with a swing of her scythe, she could snuff out any mortal life she pleased.

Aria could never understand why the living feared her. Unlike other Primordials, she wandered their world as she pleased, desperate to understand those she would one day judge. But all who saw her ran and cursed her name, beseeching their gods to protect them from the specter of death.

Eventually, a Goddess answered their prayers.

entries.name: Aria
entries.title: Petite Primordial
entries.description:
Aria doesn't remember much of her world's first True God. She's lived a long, long time, and shared too many memories to keep them all straight. But she does remember the day Orcanix first arrived. It was the day she realized she, too, might someday die.

The Undead Goddess commanded her hordes to hunt down and destroy the Primordial of Death, the one power capable of opposing her. Aria begged the mortals for shelter, but all turned her away, too terrified of Orcanix's assassins to risk her ire.

Only one man proved brave enough to protect her. A noble lord from the petty kingdom of Sanga, who foresaw the destruction to come.

entries.name: Ryouma
entries.title: Warrior of the Nameless Beast
entries.description:
Those who carry the royal blood of Sanga inherit two vows: a vow to protect their homeland, and a vow to guard the shrine of the Death Primordial.

Since birth, Ryouma has borne these vows, not that anyone knows. As a bastard, his blood is as much a secret as the stone door beneath the shogun's palace, the gateway between the world of the living and the dead. His mother raised him to live as a shadow, silently slipping along behind those in the light.

Perhaps, if he had inherited his father's wisdom instead of his pride, he could have obeyed her. But Ryouma was born a warrior through and through, and thus he sought one day to hunt the dragon with no name.

entries.name: Ryouma
entries.title: Warrior of the Nameless Beast
entries.description:
The ancient dragons of Erden bore grand names: Fornaxos, Kaidaros, Altaireon, Diabolos. All were silenced when the Goddess came, never to be spoken again. Only the dragon who slithered between the stars survived to see the new world.

Ryouma located this beast in his mountain lair, determined to prove his blade could best a dragon. He gave the beast a good fight, but in the end, could not overcome his nimble grace and speed. Satisfied with his effort, he submitted himself to a warrior's death, certain no one he left behind would miss him.

The dragon, having glimpsed the young warrior's true potential, refused to kill him, and instead made him his apprentice.

entries.name: Ossia
entries.title: The Hollow Heart
entries.description:
"Specimen name: O55-1A
Status: Stable

Specimen took her first non-assisted steps this morning. Her language capability far outpaces her physical development: she repeatedly asked for aid walking around the laboratory, and stumbled more than once. Still, this represents significant progress on her part. The faster she achieves full maturity, the better.

I've gotten word from associates on the mainland that the Aeon Lords have been sighted once again. No news on whether it has officially begun, but I fear I am running out of time. I have only just produced a living homunculus. How will she take to her powers when she finally discovers them?

I can only pray for good fortune. A thing I must do far too often, for a scientist."

entries.name: Ossia
entries.title: The Hollow Heart
entries.description:
"Specimen Name: O55-1A
Status: Critical

Another blackout today. The specimen asked, as she often does, for a chance to venture outside the laboratory. I denied her and gave the usual reasons when asked why, but upon hearing them, her mana levels spiked. She broke a quarter of the equipment in her room before her power finally overcame her. I have dosed her with a memory poultice again. Let us pray that it continues to work.

I am starting to regret this iteration of the experiment. The specimen possesses immense strength, but cannot control her emotions. Integrating her into the Pandemonic system is crucial, but doing so in this state might spell doom for us all."

entries.name: Kau Lana
entries.title: Tidewing Raptor
entries.description:
In the native Tuathan tongue, Kau Lana translates to 'born of the wind and waves.' One can easily see how these dragons earned such a name. Equally agile in the sky and the sea, the Kau Lana struck fear into the ancient Tuathan islanders, who rarely ventured out to sea despite their reliance on it for food.

Fortunately, not all Tuathans cowered before the dragons. According to legend, a chieftain by the name of Kaleiha ascended Tuatha's volcano and battled the largest Kau Lana until, at last, it bent to his will. Since then, the two races have lived in peace, working together as dragonriding pairs to nurture and protect their home.

entries.name: Kau Lana
entries.title: Tidewing Raptor
entries.description:
Unfortunately, Tuatha's prosperity could not last forever. Eighteen years ago, its central volcano erupted after centuries of dormancy, burning the village to the ground. As a gesture of goodwill, Luther invited all the surviving villagers to join him in Rigland's capital. Naturally, he adopted the Kau Lana into the ranks of the Red Dragon Knights as well.

But the children of the wind and waves couldn't thrive so far from the sea. Year after year, their population dwindled, as the dragons rejected their new training and fled to the ruins of their island.

Some say those who sail near Tuatha these days can hear their echoing cries, mourning the home they lost.

entries.name: Timia
entries.title: Captain of the Guard
entries.description:
Some men go to war for glory. Others for love of battle. Timia joined the war to follow in her father's footsteps, to see his dream of a united Erden fulfilled.

When she was but a child, he showered her with tales of the Bergarian royalty. The early tribal kings who shepherded their people through the mountains. The first true emperor, born of the Primordial of Light. They fought with true nobility, liberating the land from the tyrants who would divide it.

Their legends planted the seeds of courage in her heart. And when her father fell in battle, those seeds burst into bloom.

entries.name: Timia
entries.title: Captain of the Guard
entries.description:
Despite her youthful idealism, Timia is no fool. She knows all too well the cost of war. In her military training, her superiors made her memorize the casualties of past battles. They led her unit to rebel villages, forcibly crushed to keep their people under Imperial rule. In every eye, she saw the pain of her father's death, and she came to a resolution.

She could not allow this to continue.

As long as divisions among the people exist, there will be war. As long as there is war, innocent blood will flow. For the sake of lasting peace, the other lands of Erden must fall. And until their people understand that, Timia will do what she must.

entries.name: Clarice
entries.title: Venomous Rose
entries.description:
Once upon a time, a little girl named Clarice lived with her mother on the streets of a very cold, very cruel city.

Clarice loved her mother dearly, but love could not fill their hungry bellies. Day after day, her mother would lead her around, asking for coin, for shelter, for anything.

No one could give her what she asked, because no one had anything to spare.

Finally, on a night when the wind cut like knives, she abandoned her daughter and never once looked back. What a bad little girl she must have been, for her own mother to leave her to die.

entries.name: Clarice
entries.title: Venomous Rose
entries.description:
Clarice found her way to the mansion of a lord. For a long, long time, the lord kept her as his servant, but she was so clumsy he sold her away, to a noble couple who needed a handmaiden for their daughter Ludmilla.

Even though she was a terrible maid, Ludmilla showed Clarice great kindness. She let her borrow her books and do her hair, and over time, they became the best of friends.

Until she grew up and didn't want her anymore. Until she tried to run away.

Why would she do that? Weren't they friends? Was she a bad girl again?

Why... why... why why why whywhywhywhywh—

entries.name: Saya
entries.title: Solitary Star
entries.description:
The Goddess works in mysterious ways. Even with her lifelong apathy toward religion, Saya knows this to be true. Why else would they need the Requiem every hundred years? Not even the Church can explain just how the blood of ninety-nine innocent girls revives the world, but somehow it does, every century since the dawn of history. 

Truthfully, Saya only cared about the Requiem because some old composer chose to turn it into a ballet. Her teacher cast her as the Goddess's Grace, the lead, hoping to make her a star. 

In a way, she did. Soon after the performance, the whole world knew her name. But not for the reasons Saya had hoped.

entries.name: Saya
entries.title: Solitary Star
entries.description:
It happened so fast. Her car dove off the bridge and crashed into the water below, submerging her, drowning her. It should have been her end, a rising star snuffed out by tragedy. For better or for worse, it wasn't. 

Deep beneath the river, Saya escaped the wreckage and rose light as air, buoyed by the burning power inside her. Something would not allow her to die. Not yet. Not before she'd served her purpose. 

As she soon learned, the Vessels of the Goddess cannot die before the Requiem. The shard of divinity inside her, the Goddess's Grace she'd shown through her dance, had protected her from death. 

But at what cost?

entries.name: Hibiki
entries.title: Demon of Class 2-B
entries.description:
The Church keeps records of the order they find the Vessels for the Requiem. In truth, it doesn't matter all that much. Some Vessels found early have always been weak, while a few Vessels found late have proven themselves to be contenders. 

Still, everyone knows Hibiki Himeno was the very first Vessel found. Even worse, no one knows her Gift. 

Speculation floats around, of course. The Goddess's Bloodthirst would explain her ferocity in battle. Wit would explain her ability to ace tests without studying. Some have suggested she's the Goddess's Luck, because who else could get away with half of what she does? 

But some say they saw her shadow shortly before they themselves awoke as Vessels. And no one can explain that.

entries.name: Hibiki
entries.title: Demon of Class 2-B
entries.description:
In the past, Vessels have never managed to turn eighteen. As soon as the hundredth one is found, the Requiem begins soon after, and the world enjoys another century of peace. This has always happened within eighteen years, but so far it's been almost twenty, posing the school a unique problem: graduates who were never meant to graduate. 

Some, like Nanami and Reina, proved skilled enough to become staff at the school. Others remain in a small village nearby, working odd jobs and pretending they've escaped their death sentence. 

Hibiki, on the other hand? She's only a second year, despite being eighteen, and she likes it just fine. It's not like she has to worry about the future, after all.

entries.name: Nanami
entries.title: Fleetfoot Samurai
entries.description:
Lightning Hare Nanami, Shinano Academy graduate and interim professor of combat. Vessel of the Goddess's Speed. 

That's how the other students know her, at least. To tell the truth, it weirds her out to think of herself as a professor. She'd always been a wild kid, right from the day they brought her to the school, and she can't say teaching high school girls how to beat each other up was her lifelong dream. But it hasn't been working out too badly...so far, at least. 

Everyone in the school knows her gift. With sword strikes so swift they're barely visible, there was never any hiding it. Some would say that puts her at a disadvantage. Nanami says anyone who wants to take her on is more than welcome to try.

entries.name: Nanami
entries.title: Fleetfoot Samurai
entries.description: Most of Nanami's students think her ears gave her away as a Vessel. And they're not totally wrong: a little girl with bunny ears will naturally draw more Church attention than her normal classmates. But even non-Vessels can gain an animal's ears or tail, if the Goddess chooses to bless them with it. No. Five-year-old Nanami saw a car hurtling towards a puppy in the road and dove out to save it. In a matter of seconds, she had not only reached the puppy and picked it up; she'd shot across the street, clear into the next neighborhood, too panicked to stop running. They found her downtown a day later, still holding the puppy. That was when the Church knew she was truly special.

A Christmas spirit with a mysterious gift box.
Enjoys snowball fights.

entries.name: Endless Rizette
entries.title: Maid of Midwinter
entries.description:
Rizette always enjoyed helping out around the house. As trivial as that memory seems now, she holds it close to her heart. There was a time she couldn't remember anything of herself. Not her past, not her friends, nothing beyond Orcanix's latest orders. Sometimes, she's afraid she'll slip back into that horrific void.

So she remembers how it felt to tidy up her family's meager home. How her heart raced as she nursed Finn back to health. Perhaps she wasn't heroic before her fall, but she refuses to think those little acts of kindness meant nothing.

Keeping her loved ones safe and happy was the only life she ever wanted.

entries.name: Endless Rizette
entries.title: Maid of Midwinter
entries.description:
She didn't love Finn as soon as she saw him. That would be too much like a fairy tale. When he arrived beaten and broken from the attack on his village, Rizette nursed him the same way she would any new refugee. They called her the Angel of the Outer City precisely because she never turned anyone away.

Still, she couldn't deny the kinship between them, both teenagers ripped from their families by the undead. When he had no place to go, it was only natural she invite him to live with her and her brother. How could she have known where it would lead?

How could she have known their growing love would lead to ruin?

entries.name: Endless Astrid
entries.title: Twilight Bride
entries.description:
Come one, come all, to toast the dying year.

Astrid has prepared a feast for all of winter's wanderers. Her hearth burns warm and her table seats hundreds. As soon as night falls, she opens her gates and welcomes monster and innocent alike. She has been both. She knows that life flies by, no matter who you are, no matter what you've done. It is what separates the mortals from the gods.

Few view death as a celebration. But then, few can see it through Astrid's eyes.

Mortality, the chance to grow and change, is the greatest gift a soul can be given.

entries.name: Endless Astrid
entries.title: Twilight Bride
entries.description:
There was a time she couldn't conceive of joy. Even now, the feeling seems a stranger to her, a guest she invited without truly knowing. Orcanix once convinced her revenge would be her joy, but even deep in the Goddess's thrall, she knew that was a lie.

Astrid never delighted in destruction. Looking back, she wonders how she managed to build the Rebellion when her heart was so black with misery. She didn't want to hurt Ludmilla. She didn't hate Last Bastion, or its Vanguard soldiers. She just wanted to know why.

What had she done to make her friend hate her so much?

entries.name: Mirai
entries.title: Sparkling Idol
entries.description:
Even as the world crumbles, nothing draws a crowd quite as big as Mirai's.  

It only makes sense that people line up in droves to see a Vessel in person. Never mind the ugly business of the Requiem. Mirai's voice is that of the Goddess, and it's said that all who hear it are blessed.  

Indeed, reports of civil disturbance plummet in the wake of her concerts. Everyone seems just a little happier. Nothing actually changes for the people, of course. A happy song can't end a famine or turn back a flood. But it can make them forget for a time. 

It's her duty to make them forget.

entries.name: Mirai
entries.title: Sparkling Idol
entries.description:
It's not that Mirai doesn't like being an idol. In fact, singing is the greatest joy she has in life. She sang long before she was put on stage. She thought, in her early days at the academy, that if she worked hard enough, they would let her leave and travel the world. 

She thought correctly. At ten years old, the Church began training her for the demanding lifestyle of an idol. She didn't mind the rigor, nor the invasion of her privacy. After all, her life was never really hers anyway. 

Sometimes she wishes she could sing as herself, without using the Goddess's voice. Just once, to see if the people still love her. 

She likes to think they would.

entries.name: Kana
entries.title: Elysian Shadow
entries.description:
Kana Narukami and her little sister Mirai were born with cat ears and a tail, in a world that was mostly human. 

Their parents took this as a sign of good fortune. Only those especially close to the Goddess took on the traits of an animal. Both sisters shared a cat's grace, its speed, as well as its knack for survival. Every time their poor family pulled through misfortune, their mother credited it to her daughters' blessings from the Goddess. 

One day, that blessing revealed itself as the curse it truly was. 

If she'd known Mirai's voice was that of the Goddess herself, Kana would never let her sing at all.

entries.name: Kana
entries.title: Elysian Shadow
entries.description:
Men in black suits snatched Mirai off the street one morning as she sang for pennies from strangers. They told Kana they were from the Church, and thanked her for keeping a Vessel safe for so long. When Kana sank her teeth into the men taking her sister away, they knocked her out and left her there for Fate to do what it would. 

After a few hours, she woke up and did what any cat would do. She sought out food and shelter. She stayed in the shadows. She wore a mask to hide her horrid pain. 

She adapted. She survived, because she knew it was the only way she would ever get her sister back. 

And at long last, she thinks she's found a way.

entries.name: Antares
entries.title: Thunderhearted Heroine
entries.description:
Antares has always been your average girl. 

Her grades are good enough not to disappoint, but she's far from the top of the class. She's pretty in a cute kind of way, but she doesn't turn heads. She's well-liked among her classmates, but not truly popular. 

She blends in so well that at times, she's afraid she'll disappear. 

But jealousy doesn't come naturally to her, and she can never stay mopey for longer than a day or two. Whenever she starts to feel down, she just looks around for what she loves. 

Even in a dying world, she finds things that make her smile. And even if she doesn't realize it, that is a remarkable gift.

entries.name: Antares
entries.title: Thunderhearted Heroine
entries.description:
The thing about discovering your destiny is that you're never truly ready. Everyone's life is average until the moment it isn't -- and none have learned this better than Antares. 

She doesn't know if the Goddess influences fate itself, or if she's just very talented at stringing together coincidences, but as it would happen, her path crossed Mirai Narukami's totally by accident. By meeting her, she discovered the sinister plot of a foreign god looking to brainwash the world. 

And by discovering that plot, she found the power that slept inside her all along. The Goddess's Hope. 

As timid and unsure as she is, only she can save the world.

entries.name: Anya
entries.title: The Lost Von Draclow
entries.description:
In another world, Luther von Draclow inherited the Red Dragon Knights from a long line of ancestors. But in this world, the world of the Requiem, he founded the military order himself.

A brilliant man, he married an equally brilliant woman to carry on his legacy. His first daughter, Astrid, lived up to his every expectation, and he believed her future siblings could one day surpass her.

But his vision was not to be. His wife passed away giving birth to his second daughter, who he would later name Anastasia.

In every other world, she perished with her mother. Only the technology of Pandora's world gave her the means to fight her Fate. To live.

entries.name: Anya
entries.title: The Lost Von Draclow
entries.description:
Luther created the Ex Machina weapon alongside the Red Dragon Institute. Using a cloud of invisible nanomachines, an Ex Machina wielder could gain powers rivaling even those of the Goddess's Vessels. But this wasn't enough to satisfy him.

Little Anya's brain, kept in a coma while her body underwent medical treatment, proved to be more malleable than any other Luther had ever seen. He risked her life to mold it into a living quantum computer, capable of recording and even manipulating the forces of Fate.

Anya never knew exactly what her father was doing to her with all the surgeries she underwent. She only knew that if she kept surviving, she would meet her beloved sister Astrid.

entries.name: Reina
entries.title: Deadly Nurse
entries.description:
Reina Fujiwara, third child of the Akise Fujiwara family, was never meant to amount to much. Her two older brothers were heir and spare to the Fujiwara real estate empire. Her parents believed she would stand quietly in their family photos, enjoy the luxury of their wealth, and one day marry some celebrity or prince to secure them political power.

They never once thought, not until the day the Church took her away, that they had given birth to a piece of the Goddess herself.

Reina became the Tenth Vessel of Pandora at age seven, bearing the Gift of the Goddess's Wisdom. And she is happier at Shinano Academy than she ever was at home.

entries.name: Reina
entries.title: Deadly Nurse
entries.description:
Knowing her Gift, no one was surprised when Reina quickly surpassed her classmates in every academic subject. They promoted her from second grade to sixth within three months at the Academy, then sent her directly into high school a year after that. Nothing seemed to challenge the sharp little girl, who burned through every exam with masterful finesse.

Soon, Reina found herself studying on her own, taken out of the other girls' classes entirely. This was in part to spare them the pain of being so thoroughly bested, but that was not the only reason.

The teachers had noticed that tears, fights, and chaos followed wherever Reina went. For everyone's sake, it was better to keep her alone.

entries.name: Rei
entries.title: Sakura Onmyoji
entries.description: Once every decade, the sakura of Kisenkyo bloom and the Great Sage makes her appearance. Every time, she hears the whispers: "Betrayer." "Traitor." She says nothing, for she knows this is only what she deserves. Every decade, she witnesses Kisenkyo's peace, its prosperity, and their weight of her unforgiven sin.

entries.name: Rei
entries.title: Sakura Onmyoji
entries.description: The reddish light of the setting sun, the scent of flowers and trees, and her mother's laugh. This is Rei's happiest memory, from when her father still watched over Kisenkyo. To her, an outsider, her mother was her only friend. With her mother a thousand years dead and gone, Rei wondered if she would ever laugh like that again.

The Beast Shikigami's Level, Limit Breaks, Awakenings, Boosts, Potential, Mastery, Weapon and Accessory will match the user. Usable only if there is at least 1 other ally on the battlefield, and there is not already an ally Beast Shikigami on the battlefield or in allied reinforcements.

entries.name: Young Rizette
entries.title: Pure Blue
entries.description: When she was young, Rizette was a very timid girl. She would jump in fear at distant footsteps or the howls of wolves. Fortunately, her grandmother was always there for her. Her kind words never failed to bring a smile back to Rizette's face. But she never did understand the "courage" her grandmother spoke of.

entries.name: Young Rizette
entries.title: Pure Blue
entries.description:
When Rizette awakens, the sweet smell of pear pie fills the room. Half-asleep, she goes to the table, where her grandmother greets her with a smile. Theo arrives, still yawning, and the three of them start their day.
"Mm, so good!"
"Aah, hot!"
"Haha. Slow down, dear!"
"You're gonna burn your tongue again, Sis!"
She thought those days would last forever.

entries.name: Shanna
entries.title: Red Riding Hood, Dragon Slayer
entries.description: There are rumors among the townsfolk of a strange girl who lives in the forest. She dresses in an outlandish fashion and uses mysterious words, like "ebon", "abyss", "thanatos", and "wherefore". Then, she puts her hands on her hips, throws her head back, and laughs. Some people say she's a sorcerer. Others... just say she's having fun.

entries.name: Shanna
entries.title: Red Riding Hood, Dragon Slayer
entries.description: Today, as usual, she brings her beloved axe Van Helsing with her on a walk into the forest— for the doom of the Gods, the dawn of Ragnarok, draws nigh, and she must train. On that day, the powers of her cursed left eye, her evil right arm, and the darkness billowing inside her will all be unleashed... or so she says, causing her adoptive mother no end of worry.

entries.name: Luna
entries.title: Archer of the Woods
entries.description: The always-bright and cheery Luna is, to the people of the slums, like the warm golden sun itself. A crack shot with a bow, she often goes bounty hunting; and while she's stingy with money, it's all so she has more to spread among the poorest people in town. While a dark secret lurks behind her bright smile, Luna never speaks of it to anyone.

entries.name: Luna
entries.title: Archer of the Woods
entries.description: The first time she arrived in town, the suspicious and insular townsfolk turned her away, forcing her to live in the forest beyond the walls. One day, the town was besieged by bandits, and Luna, with only her trusty bow, dispatched them all. The townspeople were overjoyed, thanking her over and over. It was the first time she'd ever been accepted in her life.

entries.name: Snow White
entries.title: Queen of Dreams
entries.description: She has always been alone. Born in a snowy kingdom, she was kept imprisoned in a single room of a castle by her stepmother. Even when she was abandoned in the woods as a child and adopted by her new mother, she was unable to go outside. The outside world, framed by her window, has always been faded in shades of black and white.

entries.title: Queen of Dreams
entries.description: She was kinder and more pure than anyone. No one would doubt that. Yet within her, a pitch-black evil ate away at the snowy white of her heart. When it finally reached the tipping point...

entries.name: Ludmilla
entries.title: The Paladin Bride
entries.description:
Even the flower petals dance, as if in celebration.
"Wait! I have no intention of marrying. This is just a test fit."
A pure white dress and long locks of raven hair. Her cheeks color as pink as the flower petals as she asks, "well? How do I look?"
Someday, someone may have the honor of answering her in truth.

localization.name: Shield of Aldyne
localization.description: The user cannot be defeated for 100TU. Heals all allies on the battlefield for HP equal to 600% of the user's Attack. All allies are granted the Armor status effect. This will overwrite other status effects on those units. Armor grants 50% damage reduction from the next attack, then the effect is removed. Usable once each time this unit is on the battlefield.

localization.description: If this unit takes damage from an attack and survives until after the end of the turn, this unit will heal for 10% of max HP. This unit is a Guardian. For any enemy attack that would hit 1 or 2 other allies, this unit has a 100% chance to redirect 1 hit to itself instead. This unit will not redirect skills that don't deal damage.

entries.name: Ludmilla
entries.title: The Paladin Bride
entries.description:
The sun fills the air with a golden light as she turns back. "Hurry, dear, come on."
A past that may have been, or a future that may yet be. Even after she lays down her mace, she will surely continue protecting those she loves - her husband, her children, her family.

entries.name: Clarice
entries.title: Black Summer Rose
entries.description:
"Kuhuhu. How do I look, Miss? It suits me? My, how wonderful to hear."
"Since we have the chance, would you do me the honor of playing with me? You would!? Then please, assist me in making a boat. Once we have, we can row to a deserted island, then send the boat out into the sea. Then it will be just you, me, and our love, forever."

entries.title: Black Summer Rose
entries.description:
"Miss! Would you care for a drink?"
"Ah, this other one? Mm. You, over there. If you must stare so enviously, then here, have it before the ice melts."
(How rude of you, to intrude upon my private time with my Mistress. For all you know, someone might have put something in that. Fortunately for you, that isn't the case... this time. Kuhuhu...)

entries.title: Bashful Bunnygirl
entries.description: Rizette spent the night before the trip to Orka worrying. About what? The request from Callen, of course. Even imagining wearing a swimsuit was mortifying. So, she picked the one that showed the least amount of skin... but a monster attack and a perverted frog had other ideas.

entries.description: Rizette, moved by the plight of a young girl, agreed to take the place of her imprisoned parents. She was told her role would be as a "waitress", but what she got was a "bunnygirl" outfit. Still, the girl's tears moved Rizette to press forward. Though she felt nothing but apprehension, she was greeted with a loud cheer as she stepped onto the floor.

entries.title: Seaside Scholar
entries.description: Callen's workaholic tendencies didn't lend themselves well to a balanced diet, leaving her snacking on chips and candy rather than proper meals. Once, she was taunted by Druke for her eating habits. "I-I can handle this just fine!", she insisted, popping Brussels sprouts into her mouth. She passed out and it took half an hour for her to wake back up.

entries.name: Dark Jeanne
entries.title: Maiden of Darkness
entries.description:
She was the hero of her country. She was a saint to all the Followers of Light - until the very people who adored her betrayed her and condemned her to death.

Now, she rises again from the ashes. Her banner flies high, no longer a symbol of light, but of darkness.

entries.description: Mirai has a special song that she sings only once a year. No matter how cruel the world may be, on that day, she wants everyone to enjoy candy and dressing up in strange costumes. She wants to give them a chance to smile.

entries.description: When her concert is finally over, Mirai stealthily goes to enjoy the festival. After growing up in a poor home, the endless rows of candies seem like a dream to her. A little embarrassed, a little happy, she takes as much as she likes.

entries.name: Mysha
entries.title: Fox-Eared Blader
entries.description:
Let's go!
Just like always, Mysha can be seen swinging her greatsword, kicking up gusts of wind. Someone once asked her if she didn't get bored doing the same thing every day.
"Of course not! It's totally different every time. I was faster yesterday than the day before. I'm stronger today than I was yesterday. And I'll be even better tomorrow!"
She keeps on striving toward the pinnacle.

entries.name: Mysha
entries.title: Fox-Eared Blader
entries.description:
She may not remember her birth parents well, but she grew up with Gilgan, so she's able to keep smiling.
She may not be kud, but the people of Urg Roa accepted her all the same, so she's able to keep smiling.
She's faced many hardships, but she's been blessed with happiness above and beyond the dark times. "That's why I want to keep smiling forever!"
"Konkon! May good fortune, safety, and happiness be with you always!"

While little is known about the isolationist region of Outer Eastland, the White Snake Onmyoji is widely rumored to be one of the four elite onmyoji who protect Outer Eastland from the Pandemonium.

entries.name: Mizuchi
entries.title: White Snake Onmyoji
entries.description: In a parallel world where society has fully embraced science and technology, concepts such as "onmyodo" and magic are relics of the past. However, in this world, she is one of the few people called "Superhumans", people with supernatural abilities that would be considered magic in Erden. While many Superhumans tend to avoid the limelight, she is very outgoing and has no fear of being seen in public.

entries.name: Hibiki
entries.title: Scarlet Oni Samurai
entries.description:
In a land far to the East, there lives an oni. This oni, however, is a slayer of her fellows.
She prays to no gods, performs no rituals. Yet when battle looms, she is the first to ready her blade. She is the Scarlet Oni, the one who strikes at her fellows' throats without mercy.
"I'm Hibiki, the oni hunter. Eh? Why's an oni hunting oni? ...Say that one more time and I'll take your head clean off."

entries.name: Hibiki
entries.title: Scarlet Oni Samurai
entries.description:
Hotter, hotter, hotter. Blood seeks, craves blood. The fire within her rages outward, onward. It burns those her crimson gaze passes over. In her nightclothes, she can even hear it— the pounding rush of her heart pumping her burning blood.
Oni blood, irresistibly pulling her along a path she has no choice but to walk.
"Your blood's red, same as mine..."

entries.name: Ludmilla
entries.title: Knight of the Holy Eve
entries.description:
Inside the castle, the party continues, with food, chatter, and the clink of glasses. But Ludmilla finds herself outside, alone, her cheeks and nose reddened by the cold. A clinging dress, uncomfortable high heels... "What am I doing in these? They don't suit me at all..." Her sigh is white mist in the frozen night.
"That's not true at all, Miss." Hearing a soft voice and footsteps behind her, she turns.

Ludmilla sighs as Clarice pours her another flute of champagne. "A Marshal should never drink on duty..."
"You aren't on duty now. Why not indulge, just for tonight?" Ludmilla can't argue with that, and picks up her glass. Pleasant drink and pleasant company while the hours away.

That's because the famed genius looked like she couldn't be older than ten.

entries.name: Jeanne d'Arc
entries.title: Valentine's Day Maiden
entries.description:
One day earlier:
"Mikaela! You need to measure the sugar before adding it!"
"Nah, it'll be fine."
"It won't! It'll taste weird!"
"So what? It's just a customary gift, doesn't need to be perfect."
"Yes, b-but..."
"Ahaha, just kidding. Don't worry, your big sis has your back♪"
"Ugh, you're so mean...!"

entries.description: Sakura Hayate has never been a very good singer. She's not a particularly skilled dancer either. Still, she sings, and dances, and never stops smiling. Just to see that bright smile, thousands of fans are ready to wait in line for hours before her every performance.

entries.name: Sakura
entries.title: Thoroughbred Idol
entries.description: Before the show begins, she takes a moment to tell herself she's going to be okay. Though she seems so cheerful and carefree when on stage, she's the hardest working member of the group, always staying in the rehearsal room long after the others have gone home. This earnestness is what makes her who she is, but it sometimes makes her feel like the pressure of it all is about to crush her.

entries.name: Astrid
entries.title: Red Dragon Bride
entries.description:
Underneath the cloudless sky, Astrid appears in her finest attire yet, causing her secret admirers among the commoners and soldiers alike burst into deafening applause. However, she herself doesn't seem pleased with her outfit.
"I have been told this is going to be the presentation of a new combat uniform... They set me up...!"

entries.name: Astrid
entries.title: Red Dragon Bride
entries.description:
"Looking good, Astrid!" a young man tells her after the ceremony.
"How insolent! You think you can mock me just because of this unbecoming outfit?" she snaps, face red with anger.
"No, I'm being serious!"
"You obviously are not! I won't let you get away with it!" she says but the man finds her embarrassment charming. "And what are you smiling at now, you oaf!?"

"Alright, everyone! Are you ready!? Konkon∼♪"
From the moment she arrives on stage as the live starts, the fans' cheers never die down. She was born to be an idol, in some ways more so than Mirai or Sakura, because no-one enjoys their performances like she does—not even the audience.

entries.title: Fox-Eared Idol
entries.description: Mysha's stamina seems to be endless. She never runs out of breath during dance lessons, and is just as lively after an exhausting performance as she was before. During the yearly Idol Sports Day event, her team is always sure to win. And when she runs with all her energy and enthusiasm on display, the audience just can't look away.

entries.name: Shuten-Douji
entries.title: Degenerate Oni Queen
entries.description:
Tonight, as the moon brushes stray clouds away, its light falls on the beautiful cherry blossoms that mark the end of winter—ah, no, no. It falls on a bewitching oni monarch, whose arrival marks the end of the human world. Those who dare take but one look at her can smell a flowery scent in the air, can hear laughter ringing out like a bell.
In the end, they get to know why at times cherries bloom in red.

entries.name: Shuten-Douji
entries.title: Degenerate Oni Queen
entries.description:
Touch repaid with destruction, smile—with fear, trust—with betrayal. There are only those who have power and those who don't. Those with horns and those without them. And in both cases, a world in which the latter outnumber the former was one too weak for the Oni Queen's liking, too small. She isn't kind about it—she'll be out tonight as well, destroying, devouring, amusing herself, as the mysterious Shuten-Douji should.
Makes you wonder what came first—the stones being thrown at the mere sight of horns, or the oni girl wishing for freedom.

entries.description: Wherever she went, life followed—barren earth sprouted flowers, withered trees burst into leaf, villages exhausted by drought came alive again. Before long, people started calling her a goddess—Gaia, the Goddess of the Earth.

entries.description: No one knew where she came from or where she would go next; no one really knew her at all. People tried to stop her from leaving at times, but she'd still disappear, leaving behind only a fleeting smile. The desire to keep her grew into obsession, bred conflict, stained the earth with blood. And so she stopped showing up altogether.

There were some who saw her burning, crimson eyes, her beauty like a flower blooming on the battlefield, and thought her a god too.
Still, depending on who looks, a god might just as easily be a demon.

The foxfire burns — an unreachable flame, always flickering, and eventually turning dark.
Like countless hands inviting you to join them in the shadows.

Whatever you do, do not wake her up.
They say that this demon has once disguised herself as a beautiful girl and devoured people throughout the capital. Finally, thanks to the courage of a group of heroes, her spirit was sealed and peace descended on the city once again.

And I did not do it for you, I did it for myself—" She flounders for a minute, before calming herself down. "Hmpf, forget it. Now, come here already!"

"Hohoho, what is it? You turned awfully red all of a sudden. Has my outstanding beauty rendered you speechless?" Eager to get a reaction, Callen attempts to strike an alluring pose on the piano bench. However, her unathletic body struggles to keep up and she promptly loses her balance. 
"Eeeek! H-Hey! What are you gawking at? Help me up, you idiot!"

entries.name: Rei
entries.title: Sakura Bride
entries.description: After countless centuries spent in a gloomy magical realm, Rei finds herself dazzled. The bright light filling the church, the sparkling raindrops scattered over cherry blossoms, the perfectly clear blue sky—it's almost too much. But it is all meant to celebrate her, and before she knows it, she has stopped in the middle of the aisle, overwhelmed with emotion.

entries.name: Rei
entries.title: Sakura Bride
entries.description:
There are unexpected, familiar faces among the guests, and Rei's eyes go wide in surprise. The bikki from Kisenkyo have come, and they are calling out to her from the distance, not a hint of strain in their smiles.
"You look so beautiful, Sage Rei!"
It feels as if her past sin and their years of resentment all have vanished, just for today. As Rei waves back at them, her eyes well up with tears.

entries.name: Snow White
entries.title: A Midsummer Snow's Dream
entries.description: Snow White has never seen the sea before. Raised in a dark forest on the outskirts of a small kingdom, she's only ever heard of things like the summer sun and the azure sea in bedtime stories. "S-Sorry I made you wait," she says, nervousness mixed with excitement in her voice. When your eyes meet, she makes a peace sign with both hands to hide her embarrassment. "Let's make a lot of good memories today!"

"What a revolting season..."
Unexpectedly, Snow Black manifests to take control. However, she seems to have emerged at the wrong moment—she immediately hides from the sun underneath a beach umbrella, loudly voicing her displeasure. Evil as she is, even she cannot bring herself to act in uncomfortable conditions.
"What are you waiting for? Hurry up and help me put on some sunbathing oil."

Here, among the charred ruins of his hometown—the place where they once led such peaceful lives—he makes his vow. To protect the white flowers that started blooming here again. To become a full-fledged Blue Dragon Knight, one that can defend his beloved sister.

In his hands even the plainest ingredients turn into the finest cuisine. "I'm sorry I can't do much more yet," he says modestly, and Rizette's heart swells with pride.

entries.description: Love and war—these two aren't opposites. Sometimes people fight because of love, sometimes love is born from fighting. And sometimes, conflict is an irrefutable proof of love.

entries.description: The red-headed goddess's love burns vehemently, beautifully. What she loathes the most is a cowardly heart. In her eyes, there is no expression of love beyond mutual struggle.

Her aura alone can separate darkness from light; her brush draws the line between this life and the next one. And her smile can cleanse whatever impurity dwells in your heart.

entries.description:
She likes peace and quiet.
She likes reading.
This place, bathed in sunlight, filled with the scent of tea, is like a sanctum of sorts. When she sits here, engrossed in her books, no-one dares interrupt her, but if you take a look every now and then, you may see her blushing. Because the books she prefers are all about love.

entries.name: Ossia
entries.title: Bewitching Devil
entries.description:
The autumn harvest festival. Children flock to the streets, dressed as evil spirits to protect themselves from the real evil. A single grown woman can be seen among them, however, her body and mind differ greatly in age. Though an adult in appearance, in spirit she's no different than the children pestering passers-by for treats. It is no surprise, then, that she too wanted to wear a costume—even if she couldn't quite find the right size.
"How do I look, Mama? If you don't say 'cute', I will play a trick on you...!"
